findings.description: After a successful token refresh, the 401 retry response is returned raw: `if (refreshed) return safeFetch(url, init);` bypasses handleClientErrors entirely. If the retried request comes back 401 again (refresh returned a token the backend still rejects), the session is NOT cleared, so RequireAuth never redirects and the user is stuck with failing requests. A 403 on the retry also won't dispatch the `permission-denied` event, and a 429 won't be normalized to a RATE_LIMITED ApiError. Tests in lib/http-client.test.ts only cover the retry-success and refresh-failure paths, not a second 401/403/429 on retry.
findings.suggested_fix: Pass the retry response through a limited error handler, e.g. add an `isRetry` flag: on retry, handle 403/429 normally, and on a second 401 call tokenStore.clear() and throw ApiError(UNAUTHORIZED, "Session expired", 401) instead of returning the raw response (no further refresh attempt to avoid loops).

findings.description: getStore() dereferences `globalThis.sessionStorage` / `globalThis.localStorage` directly. In browsers where storage access is denied (Chrome with 'block all cookies', sandboxed iframes), the property access itself throws a SecurityError, so getItem/setItem/removeSessionItem propagate the exception and crash callers — contradicting the docstring's claim that blocked storage 'degrades gracefully instead of throwing'. Note lib/auth/refresh-token-storage.ts correctly wraps every access in try/catch, but this module does not.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the storage property access in getStore() in a try/catch and return undefined on throw (and wrap removeSessionItem's removeItem call too).

findings.description: isoToDateInput() converts via `d.toISOString().split("T")[0]`, which yields the UTC calendar date, while formatDate()/formatLocalizedDate() in the same file use local-time getters (getDate/getMonth). For a UTC+7 deployment (Thailand), any timestamp from 17:00Z onward displays one date in read views and the previous date in `<input type="date">` edit fields — an off-by-one between display and edit of the same record.
findings.suggested_fix: Build the date-input value from local components: `${d.getFullYear()}-${String(d.getMonth()+1).padStart(2,"0")}-${String(d.getDate()).padStart(2,"0")}` so it matches the local-time formatting used elsewhere (and update the UTC-based expectations in lib/__tests__/date-utils.test.ts accordingly).

findings.description: readErrorMessage() returns `body?.message` typed as `string | undefined` with no runtime type check, then line 216 calls `message?.toLowerCase()`. NestJS-style backends commonly return `message` as a string array for validation errors; if a 401/403 body ever carries a non-string message, `.toLowerCase` throws a TypeError that masks the real auth error and skips the refresh/permission handling path entirely.
findings.suggested_fix: In readErrorMessage, return the message only when it is a string (e.g. `return typeof body?.message === "string" ? body.message : Array.isArray(body?.message) ? body.message.join(", ") : undefined;`).

findings.description: logout() returns early (`if (!accessToken) return;`) when the in-memory access token is absent, even if a refresh token was present. clearSession() wipes it locally, but the backend /api/auth/logout call that revokes the refresh token server-side is skipped, leaving a still-valid refresh token on the server. This occurs whenever logout fires while the access token is empty (e.g. mid-refresh, or after a 401 cleared the token store but a network-failed refresh kept the refresh token).
findings.suggested_fix: Gate the server call on `refresh_token` (or either token) instead of only the access token, sending the logout request whenever a refresh token existed, with the Authorization header included only when available.

findings.description: `<html lang="en" suppresshydrationwarning>` — `suppresshydrationwarning` is a React/Next.js JSX prop that survived the migration. In a static Vite index.html it is a meaningless invalid attribute (there is no hydration in this SPA), i.e. a stale Next idiom. The hardcoded `lang="en"` also never updates for the Thai locale persisted in carmen.locale.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the suppresshydrationwarning attribute; optionally set document.documentElement.lang from the active locale in the i18n provider.

findings.description: getDeleteDescription() returns a hardcoded English string (`Are you sure you want to remove "..."?` and `Item #N`) in an app that is otherwise fully bilingual via use-intl with messages/{en,th}.json. Thai users see an untranslated delete-confirmation dialog wherever this helper is used.
findings.suggested_fix: Accept a TranslationFn (lib/i18n-schema.ts already defines the type) and build the message from an i18n key with the item label as a value, adding the key to messages/en.json and messages/th.json.

findings.description: APP_VERSION is a hardcoded literal "1.0.1-build.20260527.dbf5ae2b" (commit dbf5ae2b, dated 2026-05-27) with no generation step found in scripts/ or package.json, while changelog.json was generated the same day. The build-id/commit shown in the footer status bar (components/footer/status-bar.tsx) will silently drift from the actual deployed build unless someone remembers to hand-edit this file.
findings.suggested_fix: Generate the version string at build time (e.g. vite `define` from git describe / changelog.json current + short SHA) instead of a hand-maintained constant.

findings.description: formatMessage() renders parsed markdown links with `key={safeHref}`. If a notification message contains the same URL twice (e.g. two [view](/pr/1) links), React gets duplicate keys among siblings, triggering a console warning and potentially incorrect reconciliation.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the map index in the key (e.g. `key={`${i}-${safeHref}`}`) since the parts array is positionally stable for a given message.

If the retried request comes back 401 again (refresh returned a token the backend still rejects), the session is NOT cleared, so RequireAuth never redirects and the user is stuck with failing requests. confirmed.suggested_fix: Pass the retry response through a limited error handler, e.g. add an `isRetry` flag: on retry, handle 403/429 normally, and on a second 401 call tokenStore.clear() and throw ApiError(UNAUTHORIZED, "Session expired", 401) instead of returning the raw response (no further refresh attempt to avoid loops).

votes.reason: The claim is accurate: line 224 of lib/http-client.ts returns the safeFetch retry response raw without re-entering handleClientErrors, so a second 401 never reaches tokenStore.clear() (and refreshTokens only clears the session when the refresh endpoint itself fails, not when a freshly-refreshed token is rejected), a retry 403 skips the permission-denied dispatch, a retry 429 skips RATE_LIMITED normalization, and lib/http-client.test.ts only tests retry-success and refresh-failure paths.

The "user stuck, session never cleared" impact is theoretical: a second 401 on retry requires the backend to reject a token it just successfully issued, and even then every caller checks res.ok and surfaces the error while the next request re-enters handleClientErrors → refreshTokens(), whose failure path calls clearSession() (lib/auth/auth-api.ts:96-99) clearing the token store and triggering the RequireAuth redirect — the system self-heals on the next request; the 403/429 retry gaps are one-shot cosmetic misses in a sub-second token-expiry race with no security impact (fails closed, no bypass or leakage).

Verified in lib/http-client.ts: line 224 returns the retry via safeFetch, which only attaches headers and catches network errors (lines 130-141), so a second 401 leaves tokenStore populated (no RequireAuth redirect), a 403 on retry skips the permission-denied dispatch (a realistic path: expired token + insufficient permission), a 429 skips RATE_LIMITED normalization, and http-client.test.ts indeed only tests retry-success and refresh-failure.

votes.reason: The claim is correct: in storage-denied contexts (Chrome 'block all cookies', sandboxed iframes) the globalThis.sessionStorage/localStorage property getter itself throws SecurityError, and getStore() at lib/safe-storage.ts:10 performs that access outside any try/catch (its undefined/typeof guards run only after a successful access), so getItem/setItem/removeSessionItem all propagate the exception to callers, contradicting the docstring's graceful-degradation claim — unlike lib/auth/refresh-token-storage.ts which wraps every access in try/catch.

Empirically confirmed via a vitest repro: defining sessionStorage/localStorage getters that throw SecurityError (Chrome block-all-cookies / sandboxed-iframe semantics) makes getSessionItem, removeSessionItem, and getLocalItem all throw, since the property dereference at lib/safe-storage.ts:10 precedes the guards; the path is reachable in practice because all other storage access in the app is try/catch-wrapped (so the app boots and logs in with blocked storage) and grn-form.tsx:48 calls getSessionItem in the render path, crashing the GRN route — only the route error boundary limits the blast radius.

Confirmed by execution: with a throwing storage getter (real Chrome 'block all cookies'/sandboxed-iframe behavior), getSessionItem/setSessionItem/removeSessionItem/getLocalItem all propagate SecurityError because getStore() dereferences globalThis.sessionStorage outside any try/catch (lib/safe-storage.ts:9-10, and the getStore() calls at lines 17/29 precede the try blocks), while refreshTokenStorage degrades gracefully in the same run — and the path manifests during render via the useState initializer at routes/procurement/goods-receive-note/_components/grn-form.tsx:48.

For a UTC+7 deployment (Thailand), any timestamp from 17:00Z onward displays one date in read views and the previous date in `<input type="date">` edit fields — an off-by-one between display and edit of the same record.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Build the date-input value from local components: `${d.getFullYear()}-${String(d.getMonth()+1).padStart(2,"0")}-${String(d.getDate()).padStart(2,"0")}` so it matches the local-time formatting used elsewhere (and update the UTC-based expectations in lib/__tests__/date-utils.test.ts accordingly).

votes.reason: Confirmed: isoToDateInput (lib/date-utils.ts:131) derives the date from toISOString() (UTC) while formatDate/formatLocalizedDate use local-time getters, so for UTC+7 any timestamp >=17:00Z yields a date-input value one day earlier than the displayed date; the sole production caller (pr-form-schema.ts:229 vs pr-table.tsx:84 display of the same pr_date field) only avoids it because the client save path happens to normalize pr_date to 00:00Z, which doesn't refute the code-level inconsistency.

The mixed UTC/local conversion exists, but the impact is unreachable: isoToDateInput's only call site is pr_date in the PR edit form, and every pr_date written by this app (create and edit, use-pr-form-actions.ts:118) is `new Date("YYYY-MM-DD").toISOString()` = UTC midnight, for which both the local-time read views (00:00Z → 07:00 UTC+7, same date) and the UTC-based date input agree — a ≥17:00Z timestamp can only come from a backend duplicate-PR endpoint that no frontend (this SPA or the source Next app) exposes.

Not refuted: the real path manifests the off-by-one — pr-form-schema.ts:229 seeds the edit form with isoToDateInput's UTC date while pr-table/pr-card/pr-header display the local date via formatDate, and 17:00Z+ pr_date values genuinely exist (backend duplicate-PR writes new Date().toISOString()); worse, use-pr-form-actions.ts:118 persists the UTC date back as UTC midnight, so saving an edit silently shifts the displayed date back one day (the only inaccuracy is that pr_date is a hidden form value plus read-only ribbon, not a visible <input type=\"date\">).

findings.description: Migration leftover: resolveAttachmentUrl builds '/api/proxy/...' URLs that are used directly as and (attachment thumbnails, lightbox images, download links). In the Next.js source app, app/api/proxy/[...path]/route.ts was a real server route, but in this SPA the '/api/proxy/' prefix is rewritten to ${BACKEND_URL} ONLY inside lib/http-client.ts (fetch level). Plain / requests bypass that rewrite, so: (1) S3/CloudFront mode — the relative URL hits the static host and the SPA fallback returns index.html (broken images, download link opens the app); (2) Docker nginx mode — nginx forwards '/api/proxy/api/...' verbatim to the backend, which has no /api/proxy route → 404; (3) dev Vite proxy — same 404. Additionally no Authorization header can be attached to bare / requests, so even with a correct URL an auth-protected download endpoint would 401. CommentSheet is used by PR/PO/GRN/CN/SR comment sheets, so attachments are broken in every deployment mode.
findings.suggested_fix: Resolve attachment URLs through the runtime config like http-client does: `const { BACKEND_URL } = getRuntimeConfig(); url = \`${BACKEND_URL}${fileUrl}\`` (no '/api/proxy' prefix). If the download endpoint requires the Bearer token, fetch the file via httpClient as a blob and use URL.createObjectURL for / programmatic download instead of direct hrefs.

findings.description: handleTimeChange does `e.target.value.split(":").map(Number)` then `date.setHours(hours, minutes)` and `date.toISOString()`. A type="time" input fires a change event with value "" when the user clears or has an incomplete time, producing hours=NaN → Invalid Date → `toISOString()` throws an uncaught RangeError, crashing to the route error boundary.
findings.suggested_fix: Guard the handler: `if (!e.target.value) return;` (or validate `Number.isFinite(hours) && Number.isFinite(minutes)`) before constructing the date and calling onValueChange.

findings.description: Double-submit race: the textarea Enter handler (line 724) and edit-mode Enter handler (line 459) call handleSubmit/handleUpdate directly without checking isSubmitting/isUpdating/isUploading. The Send/Save buttons are disabled while pending, but pressing Enter repeatedly while a mutation is in flight posts the same comment multiple times.
findings.suggested_fix: Add guards at the top of handleSubmit (`if (isSubmitting || isUploading) return;`) and handleUpdate (`if (isUpdating) return;`).

findings.description: buildTree uses the shared fallback id "uncategorized" for missing category/sub_category/item_group. leafIdsMap is keyed by node.id only, so when two different categories both contain an "uncategorized" sub-category (or item group), `map.set` overwrites the earlier entry — the group checkbox state (getCheckState) and toggleGroup for one category's Uncategorized node then operate on the OTHER category's products. Expand/collapse state (expandedIds keyed by the same id) also toggles all "uncategorized" nodes at once.
findings.suggested_fix: Make node ids path-qualified, e.g. `${catId}/${subCatId}` and `${catId}/${subCatId}/${groupId}`, so fallback ids are unique per parent.

findings.description: expandedIds is initialized once in the useState initializer from the initial `products` prop. The only consumer (routes/config/location/_components/location-form.tsx) feeds it `useAllProducts()` data that starts as [] and loads async, so the intended default-expanded categories/sub-categories never expand — the tree renders fully collapsed once data arrives.
findings.suggested_fix: Re-derive default expansion when products first become non-empty (e.g. an effect keyed on `products.length > 0` that seeds expandedIds, or track collapsed ids instead of expanded ids so the default is 'expanded').

findings.description: Migration leftover: SidebarProvider writes the `sidebar_state` cookie on every toggle, but nothing in the SPA ever reads it (in the Next.js source, the server layout read the cookie to set defaultOpen). Result: write-only cookie and sidebar collapsed/expanded state is lost on every reload (always reverts to defaultOpen=true) — a silent regression vs the source app.
findings.suggested_fix: Read the cookie (or switch to localStorage) when initializing `_open`: `useState(() => readSidebarCookie() ?? defaultOpen)`, or drop the cookie write entirely if persistence is not wanted.

findings.description: DataGridTableBodyRowCell spreads `{...(props.tableLayout?.columnsDraggable && !isPinned ? { cell } : {})}` onto the <td> DOM element — `cell` is a TanStack Table Cell object, not a valid DOM attribute. When columnsDraggable is enabled this produces React 'unknown prop' warnings and a garbage attribute. Currently latent (no caller enables columnsDraggable) but it is dead/broken code on a public layout flag.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the spread; if some dnd integration needed the cell, pass it through a documented prop on the wrapper component instead of spreading onto the DOM node.

findings.description: Stray double-quote embedded in the pinned-cell className string: `'...data-pinned:bg-background/90 data-pinned:backdrop-blur-xs" [&[data-pinned=left]...'` (same defect at line 676 in DataGridTableBodyRowCell). The token becomes `backdrop-blur-xs"` which is not a valid Tailwind class, so pinned body/skeleton cells silently lose the backdrop blur.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the stray `"` after `backdrop-blur-xs` in both className strings (lines 440 and 676).

findings.description: Stepper computes `stepsCount` by filtering children where `child.type.displayName === "StepperItem"`, but StepperItem never has displayName assigned (plain function declaration), and in real usage StepperItems are nested inside StepperNav rather than being direct children — so stepsCount is always 0. It is exposed via StepperContextValue, so any future consumer gets a wrong value. Currently no consumer uses it (verified by grep).
findings.suggested_fix: Either set `StepperItem.displayName = "StepperItem"` and document the direct-children requirement, or remove stepsCount from the context entirely (it is unused).

findings.description: Stale Next.js documentation survived the migration: the JSDoc instructs usage as `app/(root)/{domain}/error.tsx` with a `"use client"` re-export — neither exists in this Vite/React Router app (it is actually consumed via routes/module-error-boundary-adapter.tsx). Misleading for future contributors; behavior itself is fine.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the doc comment to describe the actual usage via RouteErrorBoundaryAdapter (React Router errorElement) and remove the Next.js error.tsx/reset() references.

findings.description: Hardcoded Thai string "โหลดรูปไม่สำเร็จ" in the image error fallback bypasses i18n (English-locale users see Thai). Related i18n gaps in the same zone: tree-product-lookup.tsx hardcodes English UI strings ("Product Catalog", "Search by code or name...", "Loading..."), and data-grid/columns.tsx statusColumn hardcodes "Status"/"Active"/"Inactive" while the rest of the app uses messages/{en,th}.json (keys exist under status.*).
findings.suggested_fix: Move these strings into messages/{en,th}.json and use useTranslations (e.g. errors/common namespaces); for statusColumn, accept translated labels or call useTranslations in the header/cell components.

findings.description: pageCount is computed as `Math.ceil(totalRecords / (params.perpage as number))` but ParamsDto.perpage is `number | string | undefined`. The `as number` cast hides the undefined case: if a caller omits perpage, pageCount becomes NaN, breaking pagination controls (getCanNextPage etc.). Other code (indexColumn line 73) defends with `Number(params.perpage) || 10`, this hook does not.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the same defensive coercion: `pageCount: Math.ceil(totalRecords / (Number(params.perpage) || 10))`.

findings.description: The elaborate memoization (renderInput/renderLabel/renderError wrapped in useCallback feeding a layoutComponents useMemo) is fully defeated: `handleRef` (line 109) is a new function every render and `props` (rest object) is a new object every render, and both are in renderInput's dependency array — so every memo recomputes on every render. Pure overhead/complexity with zero caching benefit. Also note the component passes both `value` and `defaultValue` through to Input unconditionally (line 476-477), which triggers a React controlled/uncontrolled warning if a caller ever supplies both.
findings.suggested_fix: Drop the useCallback/useMemo layer and render directly (the component is small), or stabilize handleRef with useCallback and stop depending on the spread `props` object.

votes.reason: The claim is correct: compute() in leafIdsMap (line 127, components/ui/tree-product-lookup.tsx) does map.set(node.id, ...) keyed by bare node.id, and buildTree gives every missing sub_category/item_group the same id "uncategorized" (lines 23/25) while creating separate nodes per parent category, so the last-computed Uncategorized node's leaf list overwrites earlier ones — getCheckState/toggleGroup then act on the wrong category's products, and expandedIds (keyed identically) toggles all Uncategorized nodes together; the path-qualified-id fix is appropriate.

The finding holds: types/product.ts (lines 47-49) declares product_category/product_sub_category/product_item_group as nullable, so the "uncategorized" fallback ids are reachable with real data, and since buildTree dedupes sub-category/group nodes only within their parent while leafIdsMap and expandedIds are keyed by bare node.id, two "uncategorized" nodes under different parents genuinely collide — map.set overwrites the earlier entry, cross-wiring getCheckState/toggleGroup (and even an "uncategorized" item-group entry gets overwritten by an "uncategorized" sub-category ancestor in the same flat map); the impact is a real correctness bug as claimed, though UI-only with no security consequence.

Not refuted: the Product type explicitly allows null category/sub_category/item_group (types/product.ts:47-49), buildTree creates distinct sibling nodes that share the literal id "uncategorized" across different parents, and leafIdsMap's depth-first map.set(node.id, ids) (line 127) lets the last such node overwrite the earlier one — so getCheckState/toggleGroup on one category's Uncategorized node provably operate on the other's products, and expandedIds.has(node.id) toggles all Uncategorized nodes at once.

rejected.description: handleTimeChange does `e.target.value.split(":").map(Number)` then `date.setHours(hours, minutes)` and `date.toISOString()`. A type="time" input fires a change event with value "" when the user clears or has an incomplete time, producing hours=NaN → Invalid Date → `toISOString()` throws an uncaught RangeError, crashing to the route error boundary.
rejected.suggested_fix: Guard the handler: `if (!e.target.value) return;` (or validate `Number.isFinite(hours) && Number.isFinite(minutes)`) before constructing the date and calling onValueChange.

votes.reason: The core bug is real and verified: clearing the time input fires onChange with value "", and `"".split(":").map(Number)` yields hours=0/minutes=undefined→NaN, producing an Invalid Date whose toISOString() throws an uncaught RangeError (the input is enabled whenever a date is selected, so the path is reachable); the only inaccuracy is the impact claim — React error boundaries do not catch event-handler errors, so it logs an uncaught error and drops the update rather than crashing to the route error boundary — but the throw and the suggested guard are correct.

The empty-value RangeError throw in handleTimeChange is reachable, but the claimed impact is wrong: React error boundaries (including React Router's RouteErrorBoundaryAdapter via errorElement) do not catch exceptions thrown in DOM event handlers, and the throw occurs before onValueChange mutates any state — so the error only logs to console, the controlled time input reverts to its previous valid value, and the route never crashes to the error boundary (no global onerror handler escalates it either).

The empty-value path does throw a RangeError (verified: setHours(0, undefined) → Invalid Date), but it throws inside a DOM onChange event handler, and React error boundaries — including the router's RouteErrorBoundaryAdapter — do not catch event-handler exceptions, so the claimed crash to the route error boundary never manifests; the real impact is only an uncaught console error with the controlled input reverting to its prior valid value (onValueChange is never called with bad data).

low.description: pageCount is computed as `Math.ceil(totalRecords / (params.perpage as number))` but ParamsDto.perpage is `number | string | undefined`. The `as number` cast hides the undefined case: if a caller omits perpage, pageCount becomes NaN, breaking pagination controls (getCanNextPage etc.). Other code (indexColumn line 73) defends with `Number(params.perpage) || 10`, this hook does not.

findings.description: NotificationDetailDialog renders the external-link button label with t("open") under the "navbar" namespace, but the key "navbar.open" does not exist in messages/en.json or messages/th.json (verified: only "navbar.shortcutOpen" exists). use-intl will log a missing-message error and render the raw key/fallback text on the Open button whenever a notification has an external link.
findings.suggested_fix: Add "open": "Open" (en) / appropriate Thai translation (th) under the navbar namespace in messages/{en,th}.json, or reuse an existing common key.

findings.description: PathBreadcrumb uses <BreadcrumbLink href={href}> without asChild. BreadcrumbLink (components/ui/breadcrumb.tsx line 45) renders a plain <a> when asChild is not set, so clicking any breadcrumb segment triggers a full page reload instead of an SPA navigation. This drops all client state and the in-memory access token (forcing a refresh-token boot cycle) on every breadcrumb click — a regression from the Next.js Link behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Render <BreadcrumbLink asChild><Link href={href}>{label}</Link></BreadcrumbLink> using @/lib/compat/link (or react-router's Link) so navigation stays client-side.

findings.description: In grid/mobile (infinite-scroll) mode the list query runs through useGridPagination while directQuery is disabled (enabled: !useInfiniteScroll). But `error` and `refetch` are taken only from directQuery (lines 189-190), and useGridPagination (hooks/use-grid-pagination.ts) does not expose any error at all. Consequently: (1) API failures in grid/mobile view are silently swallowed and the user sees the EmptyComponent "no data" state instead of ErrorState; (2) pull-to-refresh on mobile calls directQuery.refetch, which fetches the disabled single-page query whose data is never displayed, so the visible grid list is not refreshed.
findings.suggested_fix: Expose error and a reset/refetch function from useGridPagination; in ConfigListTemplate pick error/refetch from grid when useInfiniteScroll is true (e.g. const error = useInfiniteScroll ? grid.error : directQuery.error) and wire pull-to-refresh to the grid's refresh.

findings.description: The dialog advertises shortcuts that are not implemented anywhere in the codebase: "g d" (Dashboard), "g p" (Purchase Request), "g o" (Purchase Order), "g r" (GRN) and "n" (Create new). Grep across components/routes/hooks/lib finds no handler for these key sequences — only "?" and "/" are handled in this file, and Cmd/Ctrl+K in module-app.tsx. Users pressing the documented shortcuts get no response. Additionally all dialog strings are hardcoded English while the rest of the app is i18n'd via use-intl.
findings.suggested_fix: Either implement the g-prefix navigation and "n" shortcuts (a small useEffect keydown state machine with useNavigate), or remove the unimplemented entries from SHORTCUTS. Move strings to messages/{en,th}.json.

findings.description: The "/" focus-search shortcut locates the search input with the selector 'input[type="search"], input[placeholder*="earch"]'. SearchInput (components/search-input.tsx) renders a default text input whose placeholder comes from t("common.search") — in the Thai locale this is "ค้นหา..." (verified in messages/th.json), which matches neither pattern. The shortcut therefore silently does nothing for Thai users (the default locale audience).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a stable hook to the input (e.g. data-slot="search-input" or type="search" in components/search-input.tsx) and target that attribute instead of matching the localized placeholder text.

findings.description: The locale message chunk is loaded with `void load().then(...)` with no .catch. If the dynamic import rejects (classic stale-chunk-after-redeploy failure on S3/CDN hosting, or transient network error), `messages` stays null forever and the provider returns null — the entire app renders a permanent blank screen with only an unhandled promise rejection in the console. There is no retry or fallback path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a .catch that falls back to the default locale loader (or a statically imported en bundle) and/or surfaces a retry UI, e.g. load().then(...).catch(() => loadFallbackMessages()).

findings.description: HomeComponent (~1,150 lines of marketing landing page) is dead code: grep across routes/, components/, lib/ and main.tsx shows no imports of home-component or HomeComponent, and CLAUDE.md explicitly states the source HomeComponent landing was not ported ("/" redirects to /dashboard). It still ships maintenance burden and risks accidental divergence; its copy is also entirely hardcoded English.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete components/home-component.tsx (and confirm nothing transitively imports it), or register it on a route if the landing page is actually wanted.

findings.description: The audit-log warning strip is a hardcoded Thai string ("การกระทำนี้จะถูกบันทึกใน audit log · ไม่สามารถย้อนกลับได้") while every other string in the component goes through use-intl (tc/tfl). findings.suggested_fix: Move the string to messages/{en,th}.json (e.g. common.voidAuditWarning) and render it via tc("voidAuditWarning").

findings.description: The progress bar never starts from 0: idle state is width:100% / opacity:0, so when navigation begins the bar transitions width 100%→80% (it shrinks rather than grows) and, because the 8s transitionDuration applies to opacity as well, opacity only reaches a few percent during typical sub-second navigations — the loader is effectively invisible for fast transitions and visually wrong (shrinking) for slow ones.
findings.suggested_fix: Reset width to ~0% when entering the active state without transition (e.g. key the bar element on navigation start or set width 0 with transitionDuration 0ms, then animate to 80%), and give opacity its own short duration (~150ms) separate from the 8s width crawl.

findings.description: fieldKey is interpolated unescaped into a RegExp (new RegExp(String.raw`${fieldKey}\|date_range:(.+)`)). All current call sites pass safe literals like "pr_date"/"po_date", but any future fieldKey containing regex metacharacters (e.g. a dotted path) would silently change matching semantics.
findings.suggested_fix: Escape fieldKey before interpolation (e.g. fieldKey.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&")) or parse with startsWith(`${fieldKey}|date_range:`) + slice instead of a RegExp.

Verified, not refuted: the message chunks are real separate hashed network fetches (dist/assets/en-C9vAFmP0.js, th-CtTMW-wR.js from non-eager import.meta.glob), the boot-time load() at i18n-provider.tsx:45 has no .catch/retry, deploy-s3.sh uses `aws s3 sync --delete` (old chunks vanish on redeploy), and I18nProvider is the outermost wrapper in routes/app-root.tsx, so a rejected boot import leaves messages null and the whole app permanently blank — the only caveat is that a failed mid-session locale switch would not blank (previous messages persist), so the blank-screen case is limited to the initial load.

votes.reason: The finding's stated consequences are wrong in the primary case: directQuery's key [queryKey, buCode, params] is hash-identical to the grid's page-1 key (gridParams = {...mergedParams, page:1, perpage: params.perpage ?? 20} and params always carries page=1/perpage=10 by default), so the disabled directQuery is a second observer of the same TanStack Query v5 cache entry — page-1 failures DO surface via directQuery.error and render ErrorState (not EmptyComponent), and pull-to-refresh's directQuery.refetch() (which bypasses enabled) refetches that shared query, whose new data flows into useGridPagination's page===1 effect and replaces the visible items; the claim only holds in edge cases (URL page≠1 or grid scrolled past page 1, where a load-more failure stalls silently rather than showing EmptyComponent).

The finding is correct and reachable: in grid/mobile mode directQuery is disabled (never fetches, so its error is always null) while useGridPagination destructures only {data, isLoading} and exposes no error, so API failures render EmptyComponent with no global onError fallback; and pull-to-refresh calls directQuery.refetch which (bypassing enabled:false) fetches into a different queryKey ([key, buCode, mergedParams] vs grid's [key, buCode, {...params, page, perpage:20}]) and never resets the grid's locally accumulated allItems, so the visible list is not refreshed — both paths are live on mobile for the many config modules that pass renderCard.

In the standard grid/mobile flow the disabled directQuery and the grid's page-1 query share the exact same TanStack Query cache key ([key, buCode, {page:1, perpage:10, ...}], since useGridPagination spreads the same params with page=1 and perpage: params.perpage ?? 20 = 10), so directQuery.error does surface page-1 fetch failures (ErrorState renders, not EmptyComponent) and directQuery.refetch() updates the shared cache entry whose new data re-runs the grid's page-1 append effect and refreshes the visible list; only narrow edge cases (load-more page>1 errors, refresh after scrolling past page 1, desktop grid entered with URL page>1) behave as the finding claims.

findings.description: useLogout() never clears the auth session. It POSTs API_ENDPOINTS.LOGOUT via httpClient (with no refresh_token in the body) and then redirects with window.location.href = "/login", but it never calls tokenStore.clear() or refreshTokenStorage.clear(). The refresh token therefore stays in localStorage, and on the very next boot main.tsx runs refreshTokens(), which silently restores the session. A proper logout() already exists in lib/auth/auth-api.ts (clears session via clearSession() AND sends { refresh_token } so the backend can revoke it) but no UI code uses it — components/navbar/user-profile.tsx and both change-password components all use this hook. Unless the backend revokes all refresh tokens from the bare logout call, 'Logout' on a shared machine leaves a fully resumable session in localStorage.
findings.suggested_fix: Reimplement useLogout's mutationFn to call logout() from @/lib/auth/auth-api (which clears tokenStore + refreshTokenStorage and sends the refresh_token for server-side revocation), keeping queryClient.clear() + redirect in onSettled. At minimum, clear refreshTokenStorage and tokenStore before redirecting.

findings.description: All four hooks fetch static files /data/thai-provinces.json, /data/thai-districts.json, /data/thai-subdistricts.json, but public/ in this repo contains only config.json and config.sample.json — the public/data/ directory was never copied from the source app (it exists at ../carmen-inventory-frontend/public/data/). Nothing in vite.config or deploy scripts generates these files. Consumers (components/lookup/lookup-thai-{province,district,subdistrict}.tsx and routes/vendor-management/vendor/_components/vendor-address.tsx) are live, so Thai province/district/subdistrict lookups and postal-code reverse lookup are broken: 404 in dev, or on SPA static hosting the fallback serves index.html with 200 and res.json() throws.
findings.suggested_fix: Copy ../carmen-inventory-frontend/public/data/thai-{provinces,districts,subdistricts}.json into public/data/ in this repo.

findings.description: useExternalExchangeRates uses raw fetch("/api/exchange-rate?base=...") — the old Next.js API route. This URL bypasses http-client's BACKEND_URL rewrite, and no backend endpoint exists yet (documented open item in CLAUDE.md). On S3/GCS static hosting the SPA fallback returns index.html with HTTP 200, so res.ok is true and res.json() throws a parse error which is then retried 3 times with backoff (4 wasted request cycles per mount). The hook always runs when baseCurrency is set, so it cannot be turned off by deployments that know the endpoint is absent.
findings.suggested_fix: Until the backend endpoint exists, gate the query behind a runtime-config flag (e.g. enabled: !!getRuntimeConfig().EXCHANGE_RATE_API) or validate the response Content-Type is application/json before parsing, and set retry: false for non-retryable shapes.

findings.description: After a successful response, useApiMutation unconditionally calls `const data = await res.json();`. If the backend returns 204 No Content or an empty body (typical for DELETE/void/action endpoints — this wrapper backs every useDelete* hook in the app), res.json() throws, the mutation is reported as failed, optimistic list updates are rolled back, and the user sees an error toast even though the server-side delete succeeded. Other hooks in this repo defensively use `res.json().catch(() => ({}))` (e.g. use-profile.ts avatar/logo mutations), showing the empty-body case is real in this API.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `const data = res.status === 204 ? null : await res.json().catch(() => null);` before the `success === false` check.

findings.description: When `enabled` becomes true the effect pushes a sentinel history entry (window.history.pushState({ __navGuard: true }, "")), but the cleanup only removes event listeners — it never pops the sentinel. Every dirty→clean transition (e.g. user edits then saves, or component unmounts while dirty after save) leaks one extra same-URL history entry, so the user's next Back press appears to do nothing, and repeated edit/save cycles accumulate multiple dead entries. The inline comment also still refers to "Next.js's popstate handler", which no longer exists in this React Router 7 app (stale migration comment).
findings.suggested_fix: In the effect cleanup, if the current history.state has __navGuard, call history.back() (with a flag to suppress the popstate handler) or use history.replaceState to neutralize the sentinel; update the stale Next.js comment.

findings.description: useProfile() creates a BroadcastChannel plus a message listener inside the hook itself, and useProfile is transitively called by useBuCode → every CRUD/query hook instance in the app. A typical page therefore opens dozens of BroadcastChannel instances, and a single BU-switch message triggers queryClient.removeQueries + invalidateQueries once per mounted consumer (N duplicate cache sweeps).
findings.suggested_fix: Move the BU-switch BroadcastChannel subscription out of useProfile into a single app-level effect (e.g. in the ProtectedShell or a QueryClient-scoped module singleton) so it is registered exactly once.

findings.description: The effect's dependency array includes `distance` (updated on every touchmove frame) and `onRefresh` (typically a new function identity each render). During a pull gesture, all three window touch listeners are removed and re-added on essentially every animation frame. The `distance` dep exists only so the onTouchEnd closure sees the latest value — a ref would avoid the re-subscription churn. Also `containerRef` is returned and null-checked but listeners bind to window, so the ref serves no purpose beyond gating the effect.
findings.suggested_fix: Track distance in a ref (mirroring the state) and keep onRefresh in a ref updated per render, reducing the effect deps to [disabled, threshold]; either bind listeners to containerRef.current or drop the ref from the gate.

findings.description: useQuantityFormatter/useRecipeFormatter read `config.quantity_format.minimumIntegerDigits` / `recipe_format.minimumIntegerDigits` and feed the value into Intl.NumberFormat's minimumFractionDigits/maximumFractionDigits (i.e. treat it as a decimal-places count), while lib/currency-utils.ts formatAmount uses the same profile field as actual Intl `minimumIntegerDigits`. Two contradictory interpretations of the same BU config field coexist in the codebase (the hook behavior is inherited verbatim from the source app, so display output matches the old app, but one of the two readings is semantically wrong and will bite when the backend changes the value).
findings.suggested_fix: Confirm with the backend team what the NumberFormat config field means, rename the field or add a translation layer in types/profile.ts, and make both call sites consistent.

findings.description: The WebSocket connection registers with only `{ type: "register", user_id }` — no access token or other credential is sent on the wss connection (WS_URL from config.json, e.g. wss://dev.blueledgers.com:4001/ws). If the backend relies solely on this self-declared user_id, any client can subscribe to any user's real-time notifications. This is primarily a backend concern, but the frontend has the in-memory access token available (tokenStore) and never presents it.
findings.suggested_fix: Send the access token in the register payload (or as a query param/subprotocol) and have the backend validate it before binding the socket to the user_id; coordinate with the carmen-turborepo-backend-v2 team.

findings.description: createLocationSchema's z.preprocess for min_qty/max_qty/re_order_qty/par_qty checks only `v === "" || v === undefined || Number.isNaN(v)` before falling through to Number(v). When the value is null — which is exactly what the product form initializes these fields to (pd-form.tsx:97, pd-location-tab.tsx:113 set `min_qty: null`) — Number(null) yields 0, so the validated output becomes 0 instead of null. Verified empirically against zod 4.4: parse(null) -> 0, parse("") -> null. Empty replenishment thresholds are therefore submitted to the backend as explicit 0 values, which is semantically different (0 = threshold of zero vs null = not set).
findings.suggested_fix: Change the preprocess guard to also catch null, e.g. `(v) => (v == null || v === "" || Number.isNaN(Number(v)) ? null : Number(v))` for all four fields (lines 137-140).

findings.description: PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL and PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL_CHECK (lines 188-191) interpolate `urlToken` raw into the request path. This token comes from the public route param /pl/:url_token (user/attacker-controlled, React Router decodes percent-encoding), and hooks/use-price-list-external.ts also appends `/submit` for a POST. A crafted link like /pl/..%2F..%2Fsomething makes the SPA issue requests to arbitrary backend paths under BACKEND_URL after fetch path normalization. The file already contains a `toSafePathSegment` helper (blocks `/ ? # \`, `..`, encoded forms) but applies it only to the two notification endpoints — every other dynamic segment, including this user-controlled one, is unsanitized.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap urlToken with the existing toSafePathSegment: `PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL: (urlToken) => `/api/external/api/pricelist-external/${toSafePathSegment(urlToken)}`` and same for PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL_CHECK. Consider applying it to other id segments for defense in depth.

findings.description: transferHandler calls form.setValue(`${fieldName}.add`/`.remove`, ...) without `{ shouldDirty: true }`. react-hook-form's setValue does not mark the form dirty by default. Both consumers (routes/config/department/_components/department-form.tsx:140,151,160 and routes/config/location/_components/location-form.tsx:152,162) pass form.formState.isDirty into useDiscardConfirm, so a user who only moves users/products in the transfer lists and then cancels/navigates away gets no unsaved-changes confirmation and silently loses the changes.
findings.suggested_fix: Pass options on both setValue calls: `form.setValue(`${fieldName}.add`, newAdd, { shouldDirty: true })` and likewise for `.remove`.

findings.description: SR_WORKFLOW_ACTION_CONFIG is built from `wf_`-prefixed keys (wf_submitted, wf_approved, ...) with no label/className overrides. createStatusConfig auto-generates label = key.toUpperCase().replace(/_/g, " ") and looks up STATUS_CLASSNAMES[key]; there are no wf_* entries in STATUS_CLASSNAMES (constant/status-config.ts), so each entry gets label "WF SUBMITTED" / "WF APPROVED" etc. and an empty className. sr-workflow-history.tsx:35 renders config.label directly in a Badge, so SR workflow-history badges show the literal "WF " prefix and lose the status colors — inconsistent with PR_WORKFLOW_ACTION_CONFIG (constant/purchase-request.ts) which uses bare keys and renders correctly.
findings.suggested_fix: Add overrides per key, e.g. `createStatusConfig([...], { wf_submitted: { label: "SUBMITTED", className: STATUS_CLASSNAMES.submitted }, ... })`, or map the incoming action to the bare status keys and reuse PR-style config.

findings.description: createProductSchema's price field `z.coerce.number().nullable().refine((v) => v != null && v >= 0, tv("minNumber", ...))` rejects null (verified: parse(null) throws the refine error in zod 4.4) even though the schema is declared .nullable() and ProductDetail.price is `number | null`. Editing a product whose price is null and submitting without entering a price fails validation with a misleading "minimum number" message instead of either accepting null or showing a "required" message.
findings.suggested_fix: Decide the intent: if price may be null, drop the `v != null` requirement (`.refine((v) => v == null || v >= 0, ...)`); if price is required, remove .nullable() and use a proper required message via tv("required", ...).

findings.description: getModeLabels returns hardcoded English strings ("Add ...", "Create", "Creating...", "Edit ...", "Save", "Saving...") while the app is bilingual via use-intl (messages/{en,th}.json). Consumers mix it with translated strings — sr-header.tsx:138 renders `getModeLabels(mode, t("entity")).pending` and wr-form.tsx:164 uses all three labels — so in the Thai locale the entity name is Thai but the surrounding verbs stay English.
findings.suggested_fix: Make getModeLabels accept a translator (like createLabelGetter in lib/i18n/label-map.ts) and add formMode.* keys to messages/en.json and messages/th.json, or replace usages with direct t() calls.

findings.description: Three modules export a `WorkflowHistoryEntry` with mutually incompatible shapes: types/goods-receive-note.ts ({status, timestamp, user: string}), types/purchase-request.ts ({user: {id,name}, action, datetime: string, ...}), and types/store-requisition.ts which copies the PR shape but types `datetime: Record<string, unknown>` (line 11) — almost certainly a mistyping of the ISO string the PR version uses (sr-workflow-history.tsx currently just doesn't render the timestamp, masking it). The identical name across three modules also invites wrong auto-imports.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the SR API payload and change datetime to `string` to match PR; consider consolidating the entry types into a shared module or renaming them (GrnWorkflowHistoryEntry, SrWorkflowHistoryEntry) to prevent auto-import mix-ups.

findings.description: buildQueryString stringifies every value with String(value), so an accidentally passed array serializes as comma-joined and a plain object becomes "[object Object]" silently (the signature `T extends object` with unconstrained values permits this). Current callers pass scalars (hooks/use-report-schedule.ts pre-joins its array at line 112), so no live bug — but the util gives no signal when misused.
findings.suggested_fix: Constrain the param type (e.g. Record<string, string | number | boolean | null | undefined>) or explicitly handle arrays (repeat key) and throw/warn on plain objects.

findings.description: TransferPayload is defined twice with identical shape: as an interface in types/transfer.ts and as `z.infer<typeof transferPayloadSchema>` exported from utils/transfer-handler.ts. Two exported types with the same name in different modules are a dead-code/auto-import hazard; the zod schema in transfer-handler.ts appears to exist only to derive the duplicate type (no .parse usage found).
findings.suggested_fix: Keep types/transfer.ts as the single source; in utils/transfer-handler.ts re-export/import that type and drop transferPayloadSchema if it is never used for runtime validation.

Could not refute: verified with the repo's zod 4.4.3 that the preprocess at types/product.ts:137-140 maps null to 0 (Number.isNaN(null) is false, Number(null)=0); zodResolver (no raw option, @hookform/resolvers 5.4.0 returns parsed output as values) hands the transformed 0 to onSubmit, mapLocationToPayload's `l.min_qty ?? null` preserves 0, and buildItemChanges includes new rows (initialized to null at pd-location-tab.tsx:113) in the add payload — so untouched replenishment thresholds are genuinely submitted as explicit 0 instead of null, exactly as claimed.

Confirmed, not refuted: new location rows initialize min_qty/max_qty/re_order_qty/par_qty to null (pd-location-tab.tsx:113), zodResolver without raw:true passes the parsed output to onSubmit (pd-form.tsx:228), the preprocess at types/product.ts:137 converts null to 0 (verified empirically against the project's zod 4.4.3: parse(null) -> 0), and buildItemChanges + mapLocationToPayload (`0 ?? null` keeps 0) submit that 0 to the backend in locations.add/update — so empty thresholds really are sent as explicit 0 instead of null at runtime.

confirmed.description: PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL and PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL_CHECK (lines 188-191) interpolate `urlToken` raw into the request path. This token comes from the public route param /pl/:url_token (user/attacker-controlled, React Router decodes percent-encoding), and hooks/use-price-list-external.ts also appends `/submit` for a POST. A crafted link like /pl/..%2F..%2Fsomething makes the SPA issue requests to arbitrary backend paths under BACKEND_URL after fetch path normalization. The file already contains a `toSafePathSegment` helper (blocks `/ ? # \`, `..`, encoded forms) but applies it only to the two notification endpoints — every other dynamic segment, including this user-controlled one, is unsanitized.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Wrap urlToken with the existing toSafePathSegment: `PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL: (urlToken) => `/api/external/api/pricelist-external/${toSafePathSegment(urlToken)}`` and same for PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL_CHECK. Consider applying it to other id segments for defense in depth.

votes.reason: The code at constant/api-endpoints.ts:188-191 does interpolate the public-route-derived urlToken raw (toSafePathSegment is used only at lines 144/146 for notifications), the hook appends /submit (use-price-list-external.ts:132), and the %2F-encoded traversal is genuinely feasible because React Router decodes the param and fetch normalizes ../ in resolveUrl's stripped path, so the finding is technically correct.

The /pl/:url_token route is public/unauthenticated and meant for external vendors, so the realistic victim's token store is empty and buildAuthHeaders attaches no Bearer; the traversal merely lets the victim's own browser hit public ${BACKEND_URL}/api/external paths it could already reach directly (no credential exposure, no SSRF, no privilege boundary crossed, responses unreadable cross-origin), making the "arbitrary backend paths" impact defense-in-depth/theoretical rather than a real exploit.

Confirmed real: the public /pl/:url_token param is decoded by React Router's useParams and passed unsanitized into PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL/PRICE_LIST_EXTERNAL_CHECK, where resolveUrl strips the /api/external/ prefix by string-slice (no normalization) and hands the path with literal ../ segments to fetch(), whose URL parser collapses them to reach arbitrary BACKEND_URL paths — the existing toSafePathSegment guard is applied only to the notification endpoints, not here.

low.description: transferHandler calls form.setValue(`${fieldName}.add`/`.remove`, ...) without `{ shouldDirty: true }`. react-hook-form's setValue does not mark the form dirty by default. Both consumers (routes/config/department/_components/department-form.tsx:140,151,160 and routes/config/location/_components/location-form.tsx:152,162) pass form.formState.isDirty into useDiscardConfirm, so a user who only moves users/products in the transfer lists and then cancels/navigates away gets no unsaved-changes confirmation and silently loses the changes.
low.suggested_fix: Pass options on both setValue calls: `form.setValue(`${fieldName}.add`, newAdd, { shouldDirty: true })` and likewise for `.remove`.

low.description: SR_WORKFLOW_ACTION_CONFIG is built from `wf_`-prefixed keys (wf_submitted, wf_approved, ...) with no label/className overrides. createStatusConfig auto-generates label = key.toUpperCase().replace(/_/g, " ") and looks up STATUS_CLASSNAMES[key]; there are no wf_* entries in STATUS_CLASSNAMES (constant/status-config.ts), so each entry gets label "WF SUBMITTED" / "WF APPROVED" etc. and an empty className. sr-workflow-history.tsx:35 renders config.label directly in a Badge, so SR workflow-history badges show the literal "WF " prefix and lose the status colors — inconsistent with PR_WORKFLOW_ACTION_CONFIG (constant/purchase-request.ts) which uses bare keys and renders correctly.
low.suggested_fix: Add overrides per key, e.g. `createStatusConfig([...], { wf_submitted: { label: "SUBMITTED", className: STATUS_CLASSNAMES.submitted }, ... })`, or map the incoming action to the bare status keys and reuse PR-style config.

low.description: createProductSchema's price field `z.coerce.number().nullable().refine((v) => v != null && v >= 0, tv("minNumber", ...))` rejects null (verified: parse(null) throws the refine error in zod 4.4) even though the schema is declared .nullable() and ProductDetail.price is `number | null`. Editing a product whose price is null and submitting without entering a price fails validation with a misleading "minimum number" message instead of either accepting null or showing a "required" message.
low.suggested_fix: Decide the intent: if price may be null, drop the `v != null` requirement (`.refine((v) => v == null || v >= 0, ...)`); if price is required, remove .nullable() and use a proper required message via tv("required", ...).

low.description: getModeLabels returns hardcoded English strings ("Add ...", "Create", "Creating...", "Edit ...", "Save", "Saving...") while the app is bilingual via use-intl (messages/{en,th}.json). Consumers mix it with translated strings — sr-header.tsx:138 renders `getModeLabels(mode, t("entity")).pending` and wr-form.tsx:164 uses all three labels — so in the Thai locale the entity name is Thai but the surrounding verbs stay English.
low.suggested_fix: Make getModeLabels accept a translator (like createLabelGetter in lib/i18n/label-map.ts) and add formMode.* keys to messages/en.json and messages/th.json, or replace usages with direct t() calls.

low.description: Three modules export a `WorkflowHistoryEntry` with mutually incompatible shapes: types/goods-receive-note.ts ({status, timestamp, user: string}), types/purchase-request.ts ({user: {id,name}, action, datetime: string, ...}), and types/store-requisition.ts which copies the PR shape but types `datetime: Record<string, unknown>` (line 11) — almost certainly a mistyping of the ISO string the PR version uses (sr-workflow-history.tsx currently just doesn't render the timestamp, masking it). The identical name across three modules also invites wrong auto-imports.
low.suggested_fix: Verify the SR API payload and change datetime to `string` to match PR; consider consolidating the entry types into a shared module or renaming them (GrnWorkflowHistoryEntry, SrWorkflowHistoryEntry) to prevent auto-import mix-ups.

low.description: buildQueryString stringifies every value with String(value), so an accidentally passed array serializes as comma-joined and a plain object becomes "[object Object]" silently (the signature `T extends object` with unconstrained values permits this). Current callers pass scalars (hooks/use-report-schedule.ts pre-joins its array at line 112), so no live bug — but the util gives no signal when misused.
low.suggested_fix: Constrain the param type (e.g. Record<string, string | number | boolean | null | undefined>) or explicitly handle arrays (repeat key) and throw/warn on plain objects.

low.description: TransferPayload is defined twice with identical shape: as an interface in types/transfer.ts and as `z.infer<typeof transferPayloadSchema>` exported from utils/transfer-handler.ts. Two exported types with the same name in different modules are a dead-code/auto-import hazard; the zod schema in transfer-handler.ts appears to exist only to derive the duplicate type (no .parse usage found).
low.suggested_fix: Keep types/transfer.ts as the single source; in utils/transfer-handler.ts re-export/import that type and drop transferPayloadSchema if it is never used for runtime validation.

findings.description: In readOnly (view) mode, the only footer button is rendered with label tc("close") but is disabled via `disabled={isPending || readOnly}`, so the Close button is permanently inert; the dialog can only be closed via the X icon or ESC. The same pattern exists in 8 sibling dialogs: adjustment-type-dialog.tsx:260, business-type-dialog.tsx:177, certification-dialog.tsx:198, credit-note-reason-dialog.tsx:177, credit-term-dialog.tsx:225, eco-dialog.tsx:191, extra-cost-dialog.tsx:174, tax-profile-dialog.tsx:200. (Inherited from the source Next.js app, but still a real UI defect.)
findings.suggested_fix: Change the cancel/close button to `disabled={isPending}` only (readOnly should not disable closing) in all 9 config dialogs.

findings.description: After a successful update, onSuccess only calls setMode("view") and never resets the form, so the accumulated transfer deltas in form values `users.add/remove` and `products.add/remove` (built by transferHandler / handleProductSelectionChange) persist. If the user enters edit mode again and saves (e.g. only renames), the payload re-sends the previous add/remove deltas for users/products that were already applied, which can duplicate associations or fail on the backend. Same issue in department-form.tsx:174-183 for `department_users`/`hod_users`. (Behavior matches the source app, i.e. inherited.)
findings.suggested_fix: In the update mutation's onSuccess, call `form.reset(getDefaultValues(<updated entity>))` (or at minimum reset the users/products transfer payload fields to { add: [], remove: [] }) so the next edit starts from clean deltas.

findings.description: The payload sends `delivery_point_name: values.delivery_point_name`, but LookupDeliveryPoint only updates `delivery_point_id` (components/lookup/lookup-delivery-point.tsx onValueChange passes only the id; onItemChange is never wired). So when the user picks a different delivery point, the submitted delivery_point_name is the stale name from getDefaultValues (edit mode) or "" (add mode), inconsistent with the new id. View mode then renders `location?.delivery_point_name` (line 418), so a backend that persists the sent name would display the wrong delivery point. (Inherited from the source app.)
findings.suggested_fix: Wire `onItemChange` from LookupDeliveryPoint to `form.setValue("delivery_point_name", item.name)` whenever delivery_point_id changes, or drop delivery_point_name from the payload and let the backend derive it from the id.

findings.description: The `%` suffix span uses `absolute top-1/2 right-3 -translate-y-1/2` but its wrapping `<div>` (line 161) has no `relative` class, and no ancestor up to the fixed-positioned DialogContent is positioned (Field/FieldGroup/form are all static; the relative wrapper inside FieldInput does not contain this span). The `%` therefore anchors to DialogContent and renders at the vertical middle of the dialog's right edge instead of inside the tax-rate input. (Same markup in the source app.)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `className="relative"` to the wrapping div around the tax-rate FieldInput and the % span.

findings.description: Status badge text is hardcoded `{item.is_active ? "Active" : "Inactive"}` instead of the `status.active`/`status.inactive` i18n keys that all 10 sibling config cards use (via useTranslations("status")). Same hardcoding in adjustment-type-card.tsx:68. Thai locale will show English here.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `const ts = useTranslations("status")` and render `item.is_active ? ts("active") : ts("inactive")` in department-card.tsx and adjustment-type-card.tsx.

findings.description: This dialog wires zodResolver to the static `adjustmentTypeSchema` from types/adjustment-type.ts, whose messages are hardcoded English ("Code is required", "Name is required", "Type is required"). Every other config dialog uses an i18n schema factory (createXSchema(tv, tfl)), so adjustment-type validation errors will not localize to Thai, inconsistent with the rest of the module.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a createAdjustmentTypeSchema(tv, tfl) factory (mirroring e.g. credit-term-form-schema.ts) and use it in the dialog; keep the static schema only for the type inference if needed.

findings.description: The add-mode auto-fill effect depends on `exchangeRates` (line 108), so when the useExternalExchangeRates query resolves or refetches after the user has already selected a code and manually edited name/exchange_rate/description, the effect re-runs and silently overwrites the user's edits with ISO defaults and the recomputed rate. Race window is real because the external-rates endpoint is async (and currently a known-missing backend endpoint, so it may resolve late or error/retry). (Inherited from the source app.)
findings.suggested_fix: Trigger the auto-fill only on actual code change (e.g. track previous code in a ref and skip when watchedCode is unchanged), or use form.setValue only for fields the user has not dirtied (check form.getFieldState(...).isDirty).

findings.description: Dead code: `_rateDecimals` (line 71) is computed from useProfile and never used, and `const queryParams = params` (line 90) is a pointless alias of `params` used interchangeably with it in the same component.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete `_rateDecimals` and the `queryParams` alias (use `params` directly).

findings.description: Grid/mobile mode has no error path: only `historyError` from the list-mode query (line 181) is handled, while useGridPagination (hooks/use-grid-pagination.ts) does not expose an error at all. On mobile or grid view a failed fetch renders EmptyComponent as if there were genuinely no records, with no retry affordance. This silently masks the known flaky-backend scenarios the project is testing against (401/429/500).
findings.suggested_fix: Expose `error`/`refetch` from useGridPagination and render ErrorState in grid mode (mirroring the !isGridMode branch); applies to ConfigListTemplate consumers too.

findings.description: handleBulkUpdate maps over ALL of currencyWithDiff, which includes currencies whose code is missing from externalRates or whose diff is 0 (newRate falls back to oldRate at lines 134-137). Each bulk update therefore writes a rate row stamped `new Date().toISOString()` for every non-base currency, including ones with no actual rate change and ones with no external quote at all, polluting the rate history (the table is sorted by at_date:desc, so these no-op rows surface first).
findings.suggested_fix: Filter the payload to items with an actual external quote and a non-zero diff, e.g. `currencyWithDiff.filter((i) => Math.abs(i.diff) > 1e-9)` (and/or skip codes absent from externalRates), and disable the confirm button when the filtered list is empty.

The defect is real and reachable: all 9 dialogs render the readOnly-mode footer button as label tc("close") with disabled={isPending || readOnly} (e.g. currency-dialog.tsx:272-274), and readOnly mode is hit by any non-admin lacking the configuration.<entity>.update permission via config-list-template.tsx:273/564 (readOnly = !!editEntity && updateDenied), leaving only X/ESC to dismiss — no security impact, but the claimed correctness/UX impact stands and the suggested fix is safe since the submit button is independently hidden in readOnly mode.

votes.reason: Verified: location-form.tsx:424-431 wires only onValueChange (id) and never onItemChange, the payload at lines 187-192 sends values.delivery_point_name which is only ever set by getDefaultValues (stale name in edit, "" in add, per location-form-schema.ts:52/79), and view mode renders location?.delivery_point_name at line 418 — so the finding accurately describes the id/name inconsistency on submit.

The stale delivery_point_name is sent but harmless: the backend's Zod DTOs (LocationCreateSchema/LocationUpdateSchema in carmen-turborepo-backend-v2 apps/micro-business/src/master/locations/dto/location.dto.ts) only define delivery_point_id and strip unknown keys, the service persists only delivery_point_id (locations.service.ts:887,995), and all read responses derive delivery_point name from the tb_delivery_point join — so the hypothesized "backend that persists the sent name" does not exist and view mode always shows the name resolved from the saved id.

The frontend does send a stale delivery_point_name (onItemChange is indeed unwired in location-form.tsx lines 420-433), but tracing the real backend (carmen-turborepo-backend-v2/apps/micro-business/src/master/locations): the create/update Zod DTOs are non-strict and contain only delivery_point_id so the unknown delivery_point_name key is silently stripped, locations.service.ts never writes delivery_point_name (only delivery_point_id at lines 887/995), and all GET responses derive the displayed name by joining tb_delivery_point on the stored id — so the inconsistent name never persists and the wrong-name display the finding predicts cannot manifest at runtime (it is at most payload dead weight).
