findings.description: Uses tc("inactive") with tc = useTranslations("common"), but the key common.inactive does not exist in messages/en.json or messages/th.json (verified programmatically; only common.activeFilter exists). use-intl will render the raw key path 'common.inactive' in the inactive-vendor badge and log an i18n error. This was the only missing i18n key in the whole zone.
findings.suggested_fix: Add "inactive": "Inactive" to the common namespace in messages/en.json and the Thai equivalent in messages/th.json, or reuse an existing namespace that already defines active/inactive (e.g. status namespace).

findings.description: In the from-price-list wizard, editing a location's order_qty (handleLocationChange) or adding/removing locations only updates items[i].locations — the item-level order_qty, base_qty, sub_total_price, net_amount, tax_amount and total_price keep the initial moq-based values set in detailToItem(). The regular PO form keeps these in sync via PoItemComputedSync (po-item-table.tsx), but that component is never mounted in the wizard. buildPoPayload/mapItemToPayload send the stale item-level qty/amounts, so the created PO's order_qty and amounts contradict its locations (and the totals shown in StepSummary, which correctly recomputes from locations for display only).
findings.suggested_fix: Recompute item-level order_qty/base_qty/sub_total_price/net_amount/tax_amount/total_price inside handleLocationChange/handleAddLocation/handleRemoveLocation (reuse computeItemPricing from po-item-table.tsx), or recompute all items from locations immediately before buildPoPayload in handleConfirm (from-price-list/_content.tsx:242).

findings.description: handleAddPicks sets currency_id and currency_code from the picked price list but never sets exchange_rate, which stays at the EMPTY_FORM default of 1. from-price-list-form-schema.ts:40 explicitly documents 'currency_id/code/exchange_rate = set ใน Step 3 ตอน pick PL', confirming the omission. A PO created from a foreign-currency price list is submitted with exchange_rate: 1 (the manual PO form sets it via LookupCurrency.onItemChange; the wizard has no equivalent). The BrowseDialog currency object only carries {id, code, name}, so the rate is not even available to pass through.
findings.suggested_fix: Include exchange_rate in the currency data returned by BrowseDialog onAdd (from the price list payload) and set form.setValue("exchange_rate", ...) alongside currency_id/currency_code, or look it up from useCurrency by currency_id before building the payload.

findings.description: handleSubmitPo first saves a dirty form via updatePo.mutateAsync (which may add new detail rows), then runSubmitPo builds the submit details from the stale closure prop purchaseOrder.purchase_order_detail. Detail rows added during the edit session are missing from the submit payload, and rows deleted during the edit are still sent. The equivalent PR flow (use-pr-form-actions.ts doSaveAndSubmitPr) correctly uses the detail ids from the update response — the PO flow does not.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the detail list from the updatePo response (mirroring doSaveAndSubmitPr in use-pr-form-actions.ts): const saved = await updatePo.mutateAsync(...); then build details from saved.data.purchase_order_detail before calling submitPo.

findings.description: buildDetailsFromForm falls back to purchaseOrder?.purchase_order_detail?.[i]?.id matched purely by array index when a form item has no id. If items were reordered/added/removed in the form, the index alignment breaks and approve/reject/review payloads can carry the wrong detail id or an empty string "" (the final fallback), which the backend would reject or, worse, apply to the wrong line.
findings.suggested_fix: Drop the index-based fallback: filter to items with a real id (as the PR module's prepareStageDetails does with .filter(item => item.id)), or carry the detail id in the form values for every row so the fallback is never needed.

findings.description: usePurchaseRequestTemplates() is called with its default enabled=true, so every visit to /procurement/purchase-request/new fetches the full template list even when there is no template_id query param (the common 'blank PR' path). The hook supports an enabled flag (pr-create-dialog passes view === "template").
findings.suggested_fix: Pass the flag: usePurchaseRequestTemplates(!!templateId) so the templates request only fires when a template was actually requested.

findings.description: activeListHook = viewMode === "my-pending" ? useMyPendingPurchaseRequest : usePurchaseRequest is passed into useGridPagination, which calls it as a hook — so toggling viewMode swaps which hook function executes in the same hook slot across renders. This violates the Rules of Hooks; it only works because both hooks happen to wrap a single identical useQuery call. The same pattern exists in po-component.tsx:114. Any future divergence in either hook's internal hook count will crash with a hooks-order error on view-mode toggle (mobile only, since enabled: useInfiniteScroll).
findings.suggested_fix: Call both hooks unconditionally inside useGridPagination's consumer (with enabled flags, as already done for myPendingQuery/allDocumentQuery in the desktop path) and select the result, or key the mobile grid component by viewMode so a swap remounts it.

findings.description: fetchPriceLists silently returns on !res.ok — the user sees the 'no price list' empty state with no error indication (the toast only fires for thrown/network errors). Additionally the effect has no cancellation/abort: rapid prop changes (open/product/currency) can resolve out of order and setLists with a stale response.
findings.suggested_fix: Treat !res.ok the same as a thrown error (show the priceListLoadFailed toast / an error state), and guard against stale responses with an AbortController or a local cancelled flag in the effect cleanup.

findings.description: Several user-facing strings are hardcoded in English and bypass i18n: '★ Preferred' (line 77), 'Best' (line 121), 'Select' (line 175), 'Best Price' (line 339), 'Max Savings' (line 346), 'vs highest' (line 348), and 'vendor'/'vendors' (line 336). Thai users see English labels. Also minPrice (line 260) computes Math.min over a filtered array — if every price is 0 the filter is empty and Math.min() returns Infinity, which formatCurrency then renders as the 'Best Price'.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the strings into messages/{en,th}.json under procurement.purchaseRequest, and guard minPrice: const positive = lists.map(l => l.price).filter(p => p > 0); const minPrice = positive.length ? Math.min(...positive) : 0;

findings.description: Row selection is stored as a Set of array indices (selected) but is never reset or remapped when the items field-array mutates: handleAddItem prepends (shifting every index by one) and removeItem deletes (shifting subsequent indices), so an existing selection silently points at different rows. Bulk approve/review/reject would then setValue on the wrong items. The role gating (selection UI for non-draft, add for create role) reduces but does not eliminate the overlap.
findings.suggested_fix: Call resetSelection() inside handleAddItem and in the DeleteDialog onConfirm (after removeItem), or key the selection by RHF field id instead of index.

findings.description: pendingCount and handleSelectPending read item.current_stage_status from useFieldArray's fields objects. RHF field-array entries are snapshots that update only on array operations (append/remove/prepend), not on setValue of nested fields — so after bulk approve/reject/review mutate current_stage_status via form.setValue, 'select pending' still selects rows by their stale pre-action status.
findings.suggested_fix: Read statuses from form.getValues("items") (or a useWatch on items) in pendingCount/handleSelectPending instead of from itemFields.

findings.description: The footer subtotal is computed as pricelist_price * requested_qty, but net_amount/tax_amount/total_price summed in the same loop were derived (in pr-item-expand/computePrItemAmounts) using calcQty = approved_qty > 0 ? approved_qty : requested_qty. At approve/purchase stages where approved_qty differs from requested_qty, the displayed subtotal is internally inconsistent with the displayed discount/net/total (subtotal - discount ≠ net).
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the calcQty logic: const qty = Number(item?.approved_qty) > 0 ? Number(item.approved_qty) : Number(item?.requested_qty ?? 0); when accumulating subtotal.

findings.description: PoFooterAction receives currencyCode={form.getValues("currency_code")} — a non-reactive read during render. PoForm only watches "items", so when the user changes the currency in PoGeneralFields (which setValue's currency_code without touching items), the footer's currency label keeps showing the old code until some unrelated re-render occurs.
findings.suggested_fix: Watch the field: const currencyCode = useWatch({ control: form.control, name: "currency_code" }) and pass that to PoFooterAction.

findings.description: Multiple handlers call router.refresh() (lines 116, 173, 221, 246, 274, 287), but the compat router's refresh is an intentional no-op in this SPA (lib/compat/navigation.ts:53 — 'invalidate ที่ฝั่ง caller แทน'). Data freshness actually comes from the mutation hooks' invalidateKeys. These calls are dead code that misleads readers into thinking a refetch happens here.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the router.refresh() calls (the PO mutation hooks already invalidate PO_INVALIDATE_KEYS), keeping the compat layer for push/replace only.

findings.description: PO combines independent filter clauses with "," ([params.filter, poType].join(",")) while the PR list and useDataGridState combine clauses with ";" (pr-component.tsx:144, use-data-grid-state.ts). Since each clause's value list already uses commas internally (e.g. po_type|string:a,b), a comma joiner makes 'status filter + type filter' ambiguous for any backend parser that treats ";" as the AND separator. Note: this matches the source Next.js app verbatim, so it is inherited rather than a migration regression — flagging because the sibling PR module disagrees.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify the gateway's multi-clause filter contract; if ";" is the clause separator (as the PR module assumes), change the joiner to ";" here and in cn-component/grn-component.

findings.description: handleNext does setGroupedData(json.data.groups) without guarding json.data — if the group endpoint returns 200 with an unexpected body shape, this throws TypeError inside the try (caught, but surfaces a confusing raw error message instead of the intended 'Failed to group PRs').
findings.suggested_fix: Use setGroupedData(json?.data?.groups ?? []) and treat an empty/missing groups array as an error or empty-state before advancing to step 2.

confirmed.description: In the from-price-list wizard, editing a location's order_qty (handleLocationChange) or adding/removing locations only updates items[i].locations — the item-level order_qty, base_qty, sub_total_price, net_amount, tax_amount and total_price keep the initial moq-based values set in detailToItem(). The regular PO form keeps these in sync via PoItemComputedSync (po-item-table.tsx), but that component is never mounted in the wizard. buildPoPayload/mapItemToPayload send the stale item-level qty/amounts, so the created PO's order_qty and amounts contradict its locations (and the totals shown in StepSummary, which correctly recomputes from locations for display only).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Recompute item-level order_qty/base_qty/sub_total_price/net_amount/tax_amount/total_price inside handleLocationChange/handleAddLocation/handleRemoveLocation (reuse computeItemPricing from po-item-table.tsx), or recompute all items from locations immediately before buildPoPayload in handleConfirm (from-price-list/_content.tsx:242).

votes.reason: Verified: the wizard's location handlers (step-select-items.tsx:136-179) mutate only items[i].locations while item-level order_qty/base_qty/sub_total_price/net_amount/tax_amount/total_price keep the moq-based values from detailToItem; PoItemComputedSync is mounted only in po-items-grid.tsx (never in from-price-list), handleConfirm (_content.tsx:242) calls buildPoPayload without recomputing, and mapItemToPayload (po-form-schema.ts:246-287) copies the stale item-level qty/amounts verbatim into the payload alongside per-location requested_qty, so the finding is correct.

Confirmed end-to-end: the wizard's location qty input (step-select-items-product-card.tsx:143-147) only patches items[i].locations via handleLocationChange/handleAddLocation/handleRemoveLocation (step-select-items.tsx:136-179) while item-level order_qty/base_qty/sub_total_price/net_amount/tax_amount/total_price stay frozen at the moq-based values from detailToItem(); PoItemComputedSync is mounted only in po-items-grid.tsx (regular form), StepSummary recomputes from locations for display only without setValue, and handleConfirm (_content.tsx:242) → buildPoPayload → buildItemChanges (id-less items all go to "add") → mapItemToPayload sends the stale item-level qty/amounts alongside the edited per-location requested_qty, producing a self-contradictory PO payload on a fully reachable, ordinary user path.

Confirmed: the wizard's location handlers (step-select-items.tsx:136-179) only mutate items[i].locations and never recompute the item-level qty/amount fields seeded from moq in detailToItem(); PoItemComputedSync — the sole writer of those derived fields — is mounted only in po-items-grid.tsx (regular PO form) and appears nowhere in from-price-list/, so handleConfirm's buildPoPayload/mapItemToPayload (po-form-schema.ts:261-272) sends stale moq-based order_qty/base_qty/sub_total_price/net_amount/tax_amount/total_price that contradict the edited locations, while StepSummary recomputes from locations for display only.

confirmed.description: handleAddPicks sets currency_id and currency_code from the picked price list but never sets exchange_rate, which stays at the EMPTY_FORM default of 1. from-price-list-form-schema.ts:40 explicitly documents 'currency_id/code/exchange_rate = set ใน Step 3 ตอน pick PL', confirming the omission. A PO created from a foreign-currency price list is submitted with exchange_rate: 1 (the manual PO form sets it via LookupCurrency.onItemChange; the wizard has no equivalent). The BrowseDialog currency object only carries {id, code, name}, so the rate is not even available to pass through.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Include exchange_rate in the currency data returned by BrowseDialog onAdd (from the price list payload) and set form.setValue("exchange_rate", ...) alongside currency_id/currency_code, or look it up from useCurrency by currency_id before building the payload.

votes.reason: Verified correct: the wizard never writes exchange_rate (only po-general-fields.tsx in the manual form does), getDefaultValues inherits exchange_rate: 1 from PO_EMPTY_FORM, the BrowseDialog currency callback is typed/built as {id, code, name} with no rate, and build-po-payload.ts:46 submits values.exchange_rate, so a PO from a foreign-currency price list is created with exchange_rate: 1 exactly as the finding states.

Could not refute: the wizard never sets exchange_rate anywhere (only currency_id/code in step-select-items.tsx:117-120), buildPoPayload sends the default 1, the PriceList currency type ({id,code,name}) carries no rate, and the backend (purchase-order.service.ts:1221,1293) persists the client-supplied exchange_rate and uses it in calcBasePrices — so a PO from a foreign-currency price list (a reachable case the dialog's single-currency lock explicitly supports) really is created with rate 1 and wrong base amounts.

Confirmed at runtime: the wizard never sets exchange_rate anywhere (grep of from-price-list/ shows no setValue("exchange_rate")), the BrowseDialog currency payload is only {id, code, name} (types/price-list.ts:59), validation (min(0)) passes with the EMPTY_FORM default of 1, and buildPoPayload (build-po-payload.ts:46) unconditionally submits exchange_rate: 1 — while the manual PO form proves the client must supply the rate via LookupCurrency onItemChange (po-general-fields.tsx:85, 210).

confirmed.description: handleSubmitPo first saves a dirty form via updatePo.mutateAsync (which may add new detail rows), then runSubmitPo builds the submit details from the stale closure prop purchaseOrder.purchase_order_detail. Detail rows added during the edit session are missing from the submit payload, and rows deleted during the edit are still sent. The equivalent PR flow (use-pr-form-actions.ts doSaveAndSubmitPr) correctly uses the detail ids from the update response — the PO flow does not.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Use the detail list from the updatePo response (mirroring doSaveAndSubmitPr in use-pr-form-actions.ts): const saved = await updatePo.mutateAsync(...); then build details from saved.data.purchase_order_detail before calling submitPo.

votes.reason: Verified: handleSubmitPo (use-po-form-handlers.ts:180-207) awaits updatePo.mutateAsync, ignores its response, and runSubmitPo (line 164) builds submit details from the closure-captured purchaseOrder.purchase_order_detail prop, so rows added in the edit session (prependItem in po-item-fields.tsx, sent as `add` via buildItemChanges) are missing from the submit payload and removed rows' ids are still sent, whereas the PR flow (use-pr-form-actions.ts:209-214) correctly uses purchase_request_detail from the update response.

The bug is real and reachable: the PO edit UI allows adding/removing detail rows (useFieldArray prepend/remove in po-item-fields.tsx, diffed by buildItemChanges), the Submit button is shown in edit mode for role CREATE, and handleSubmitPo's dirty path saves via updatePo.mutateAsync then calls runSubmitPo which builds submit details from the stale closure prop purchaseOrder.purchase_order_detail — so newly added rows (whose ids only exist in the update response) are omitted and deleted rows' ids are still sent, unlike the PR flow (doSaveAndSubmitPr) which correctly uses ids from the update response.

Confirmed, not refuted: the creator role can add/remove detail rows and click Submit in edit mode (po-footer-action.tsx canSubmit + po-item-fields.tsx Add Item both gate on STAGE_ROLE.CREATE), and after `await updatePo.mutateAsync(...)` discards the response, runSubmitPo (use-po-form-handlers.ts:164) builds submit details from the render-time closure prop `purchaseOrder.purchase_order_detail`, so newly created detail ids are omitted and deleted rows are still sent — unlike doSaveAndSubmitPr (use-pr-form-actions.ts:203-219) which correctly uses the update response's detail ids.

rejected.description: buildDetailsFromForm falls back to purchaseOrder?.purchase_order_detail?.[i]?.id matched purely by array index when a form item has no id. If items were reordered/added/removed in the form, the index alignment breaks and approve/reject/review payloads can carry the wrong detail id or an empty string "" (the final fallback), which the backend would reject or, worse, apply to the wrong line.
rejected.suggested_fix: Drop the index-based fallback: filter to items with a real id (as the PR module's prepareStageDetails does with .filter(item => item.id)), or carry the detail id in the form values for every row so the fallback is never needed.

votes.reason: buildDetailsFromForm is only reachable via approve/reject/review actions gated to role === STAGE_ROLE.APPROVE (po-footer-action.tsx:81-86), and approvers can never add (po-item-fields.tsx:246 requires role CREATE/none), remove (po-items-grid-expanded.tsx:55 requires !readOnly, readOnly = role APPROVE), or reorder rows (no move/swap/drag in the module), while getDefaultValues seeds every form row with id: d.id from purchase_order_detail in identical order (po-form-schema.ts:190-191) — so item.id is always present, the index/""-fallbacks are dead code, and the claimed misalignment scenario is unreachable.

The index-based fallback is unreachable in any harmful state: buildDetailsFromForm only runs from approve/reject/review handlers gated to role === STAGE_ROLE.APPROVE (po-footer-action.tsx:81-86), while id-less rows can only be created via Add Item which is gated to !role || role === CREATE (po-item-fields.tsx:246); all rows seeded from an existing PO get id: d.id in matching order (po-form-schema.ts:191) so the truthy item.id short-circuits the fallback even after removals, and no reorder/insert/move capability exists in the items field array.

The index fallback at use-po-form-handlers.ts:97 cannot misfire at runtime: buildDetailsFromForm is only reachable via approve/reject/review actions gated to role === STAGE_ROLE.APPROVE (PoFooterAction), every form row is hydrated with its real detail id by getDefaultValues (po-form-schema.ts:191) so `item.id ??` always short-circuits, id-less rows can only be created by the Add Item button which is rendered exclusively for CREATE-role users (po-item-fields.tsx:246) who can never trigger these handlers, and the module has no reorder operation (useFieldArray uses only prepend/remove) — making the fallback dead defensive code rather than a live wrong-id path.

low.description: Uses tc("inactive") with tc = useTranslations("common"), but the key common.inactive does not exist in messages/en.json or messages/th.json (verified programmatically; only common.activeFilter exists). use-intl will render the raw key path 'common.inactive' in the inactive-vendor badge and log an i18n error. This was the only missing i18n key in the whole zone.
low.suggested_fix: Add "inactive": "Inactive" to the common namespace in messages/en.json and the Thai equivalent in messages/th.json, or reuse an existing namespace that already defines active/inactive (e.g. status namespace).

findings.description: Approval queue pagination is broken. `totalRecords = items.length` uses only the current page's item count, while the request is server-paginated (useDataGridState sends page/perpage with manualPagination:true). The real totals (`paginate.total` per PR/PO/SR section) are extracted in hooks/use-approval.ts `extractSection` but then discarded — `useApprovalPending` returns only `{ data: allItems }`. As a result `pageCount = Math.ceil(items.length / pageSize)` in approve-queue-list.tsx is always <= 1 (e.g. 10 items / perpage 10 = 1 page), so approvers can never navigate past the first page of pending documents, and the record-count display is wrong. The client-side doc_type filter compounds this by filtering within the already-truncated page.
findings.suggested_fix: Return the section totals from useApprovalPending (e.g. `{ data, total: pr.total + po.total + sr.total }`, or per-section totals) and use that for `totalRecords` in approval-component.tsx. If the doc_type filter must remain client-side, either fetch all sections unpaginated for this screen or pass doc_type to the backend.

findings.description: Dynamically interpolated Tailwind classes `bg-${card.color}/10` and `text-${card.color}` cannot be detected by Tailwind v4's source scanner. The classes only work if the literal string happens to appear elsewhere in the codebase: `bg-primary/10`, `bg-info/10`, `bg-warning/10`, `text-info`, `text-warning` do exist in other files, but `bg-secondary/10` and standalone `text-secondary` appear nowhere literally (verified by grep), so the Store Requisition summary card's icon tint and color are silently unstyled.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the interpolation with a static lookup map, e.g. `const COLOR_CLASSES = { primary: "bg-primary/10 text-primary", info: "bg-info/10 text-info", warning: "bg-warning/10 text-warning", secondary: "bg-secondary/10 text-secondary" }` and store the full class strings in SUMMARY_CARDS so Tailwind can see them.

findings.description: `setProductToItem` writes the whole item via `form.setValue(`items.${index}`, {...})` without `shouldDirty: true`, bypassing the Controller's `field.onChange`. RHF's dirtyFields are therefore not updated for that row. In PrtForm.onSubmit, `buildItemChanges` trusts `form.formState.dirtyFields.items` when it is defined: if the user also edited any other field through a normal input (which defines dirtyFields.items), an existing row whose ONLY change was the product selection will have an empty dirty entry and be silently excluded from the update payload — the product change is lost on save. (It only works today via the shallow-compare fallback when dirtyFields.items is undefined.) Note the credit-note equivalent (cn-item-table.tsx setProductToItem) correctly passes `{ shouldDirty: true }`.
findings.suggested_fix: Pass `{ shouldDirty: true }` to the setValue call in setProductToItem (or set each field individually with shouldDirty: true, matching cn-item-table.tsx).

findings.description: In `ManualProductCell`, when a manual product group has multiple location rows, only the primary row's product_id goes through `field.onChange` (which marks it dirty); the sibling rows are updated via `form.setValue(`items.${idx}.product_id`, value)` without `shouldDirty: true`. When editing an existing GRN, `buildItemChanges` in use-grn-form-actions.ts uses dirtyFields.items (defined, because the primary row is dirty), so the sibling rows' product change is excluded from the PATCH's update array — the backend keeps the old product on those rows while the primary row is updated, leaving the group inconsistent.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `{ shouldDirty: true }` to the sibling `form.setValue` calls for `product_id` and `product_name` inside the loop.

findings.description: After a successful update the form is never reset with the refetched server state (mutation invalidates the query and the `creditNote` prop refreshes, but `useForm` keeps the original values). The payload includes `doc_version: values.doc_version`, which still holds the pre-save version. A second consecutive edit+save in the same session therefore sends a stale doc_version (and stale per-item doc_versions via mapItemToPayload), which will fail or mis-merge if the backend enforces optimistic locking. The same pattern exists in the GRN form (use-grn-form-actions.ts onSubmit edit path sends stale item doc_versions; header doc_version is silently dropped because it is not in headerKeys).
findings.suggested_fix: On update success, `form.reset(getDefaultValues(freshData))` once the refetched entity arrives (e.g. useEffect keyed on creditNote.doc_version calling form.reset when in view mode), so subsequent edits carry the current doc_version values.

findings.description: On desktop, switching to grid view renders `CnCardList` with only the current page of server-paginated data and no pagination control (`DataGridPagination` is rendered only in the `!isGridMode` list branch; the sentinel/infinite-scroll only activates on mobile via `useInfiniteScroll = !!isMobile`). Desktop grid users can never see past page 1. The GRN list (grn-component.tsx lines 487-514) implements a third branch wrapping the card list in DataGrid + DataGridPagination for exactly this case, so this looks like an omission rather than a design choice.
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror grn-component.tsx: add an `isGridMode && !useInfiniteScroll` branch that wraps CnCardList in DataGrid/DataGridContainer with DataGridPagination.

confirmed.description: Approval queue pagination is broken. `totalRecords = items.length` uses only the current page's item count, while the request is server-paginated (useDataGridState sends page/perpage with manualPagination:true). The real totals (`paginate.total` per PR/PO/SR section) are extracted in hooks/use-approval.ts `extractSection` but then discarded — `useApprovalPending` returns only `{ data: allItems }`. As a result `pageCount = Math.ceil(items.length / pageSize)` in approve-queue-list.tsx is always <= 1 (e.g. 10 items / perpage 10 = 1 page), so approvers can never navigate past the first page of pending documents, and the record-count display is wrong. The client-side doc_type filter compounds this by filtering within the already-truncated page.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Return the section totals from useApprovalPending (e.g. `{ data, total: pr.total + po.total + sr.total }`, or per-section totals) and use that for `totalRecords` in approval-component.tsx. If the doc_type filter must remain client-side, either fetch all sections unpaginated for this screen or pass doc_type to the backend.

votes.reason: Verified in code: useApprovalPending sends page/perpage server-side but discards each section's paginate.total (extractSection returns it, hook returns only { data }), and approval-component.tsx:74 sets totalRecords = items.length feeding pageCount = ceil(items.length/pageSize) with manualPagination:true — so pagination is capped by the current page's item count and the record count is wrong; the only minor overstatement is "always <= 1" (merged 3-section pages can yield up to 3× perpage items, so pageCount can reach ~3), but the core finding stands.

Verified real: the backend gateway (my-approve.controller/service) genuinely server-paginates each section (default perpage 10) and returns per-section paginate.total, which use-approval.ts extracts but discards (returns only { data }), so totalRecords=items.length in approval-component.tsx:74 caps pageCount at the fetched page's size and DataGridPagination disables navigation — e.g. with 25 pending PRs and no PO/SR an approver sees 10 records, 1 page, and documents 11-25 are unreachable; the only inaccuracy is that pageCount can reach up to 3 (one page per section) in mixed queues, which still under-counts and still blocks deeper pages.

Confirmed at runtime by tracing the full path: the backend gateway (my-approve.service.ts) genuinely paginates each PR/PO/SR section with the page/perpage the frontend sends, useApprovalPending discards each section's paginate.total and returns only the current page's items, and approval-component.tsx:74 sets totalRecords = items.length, so approve-queue-list's pageCount (= ceil(items.length/pageSize), driving getCanNextPage in DataGridPagination) caps at 1 whenever a single section dominates or a doc_type card filter is active — e.g. 25 pending PRs / perpage 10 yields 10 items, pageCount 1, items 11-25 unreachable and the record count wrong; the only nitpick is that with all three sections populated items.length can reach 3x perpage so pageCount is not literally always <= 1, but the core defect manifests exactly as described.

confirmed.description: `setProductToItem` writes the whole item via `form.setValue(`items.${index}`, {...})` without `shouldDirty: true`, bypassing the Controller's `field.onChange`. RHF's dirtyFields are therefore not updated for that row. In PrtForm.onSubmit, `buildItemChanges` trusts `form.formState.dirtyFields.items` when it is defined: if the user also edited any other field through a normal input (which defines dirtyFields.items), an existing row whose ONLY change was the product selection will have an empty dirty entry and be silently excluded from the update payload — the product change is lost on save. (It only works today via the shallow-compare fallback when dirtyFields.items is undefined.) Note the credit-note equivalent (cn-item-table.tsx setProductToItem) correctly passes `{ shouldDirty: true }`.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Pass `{ shouldDirty: true }` to the setValue call in setProductToItem (or set each field individually with shouldDirty: true, matching cn-item-table.tsx).

votes.reason: Verified: setProductToItem (prt-item-table.tsx:42) calls form.setValue without shouldDirty (RHF default false) and bypasses field.onChange, so the row gets no dirtyFields entry; buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252) skips the shallow-compare fallback whenever dirtyFields.items is defined (which happens once any other item field is edited via register/field.onChange) and excludes rows with empty dirty entries, silently dropping the product-only change from the update payload — and cn-item-table.tsx confirms the correct pattern with shouldDirty: true; the only nitpick is that editing a non-items top-level field (e.g. name) would not define dirtyFields.items, but the described failure scenario is real.

Not refuted — an empirical repro against the installed react-hook-form 7.78.0 with faithful wiring (Controller bypassed via setProductToItem's whole-object setValue without shouldDirty, plus a registered qty input on another row) produced dirtyFields.items = [null, {requested_qty: true}], and buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252, which trusts dirtyFields when defined) silently dropped the row whose only change was the product selection from the update payload, exactly as claimed; the scenario is reachable in the ordinary edit flow of an existing template (rows have ids per getDefaultValues) and the cn-item-table.tsx equivalent confirms shouldDirty: true is the intended pattern.

Confirmed real: setValue without shouldDirty (prt-item-table.tsx:42) leaves no dirtyFields.items entry for a product-only-changed row, and buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252) excludes such rows from the update payload whenever dirtyFields.items is defined by any other item edit (qty/location/unit all go through register/field.onChange, or a row append), so the product change is silently lost on save — exactly the pattern cn-item-table.tsx fixes with shouldDirty: true.

confirmed.description: In `ManualProductCell`, when a manual product group has multiple location rows, only the primary row's product_id goes through `field.onChange` (which marks it dirty); the sibling rows are updated via `form.setValue(`items.${idx}.product_id`, value)` without `shouldDirty: true`. When editing an existing GRN, `buildItemChanges` in use-grn-form-actions.ts uses dirtyFields.items (defined, because the primary row is dirty), so the sibling rows' product change is excluded from the PATCH's update array — the backend keeps the old product on those rows while the primary row is updated, leaving the group inconsistent.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Add `{ shouldDirty: true }` to the sibling `form.setValue` calls for `product_id` and `product_name` inside the loop.

votes.reason: The claim is correct: siblings of a manual group are updated via form.setValue without shouldDirty (RHF default false) while only the primary row is dirtied via field.onChange, and since existing GRN rows with ids share _group_key (grn-form-schema.ts:245) and buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252) filters existing items strictly by dirtyFields.items when it is defined, sibling rows' product_id changes are omitted from the edit PATCH's update array (use-grn-form-actions.ts:75-78, 167-169).

Verified end-to-end: editing an existing manual GRN whose product group spans multiple location rows (rows share _group_key=product_id and each has a backend id) renders ManualProductCell enabled; only the primary row is dirtied via field.onChange while siblings get setValue without shouldDirty, and since dirtyFields.items is then defined, buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252) excludes the non-dirty sibling rows from the PATCH update array — the shallow-compare fallback never runs — so the backend keeps the old product on sibling rows, making the claimed impact real and reachable.

Confirmed via trace: edit mode loads multi-row manual groups (shared _group_key=product_id, rows with ids) into ManualProductCell, sibling setValue calls (grn-product-cards.tsx:102-105) omit shouldDirty, and buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252) takes the dirtyFields branch (defined because the primary row's field.onChange dirtied it), so id-bearing sibling rows are excluded from the PATCH update array and keep the old product on the backend.

confirmed.description: After a successful update the form is never reset with the refetched server state (mutation invalidates the query and the `creditNote` prop refreshes, but `useForm` keeps the original values). The payload includes `doc_version: values.doc_version`, which still holds the pre-save version. A second consecutive edit+save in the same session therefore sends a stale doc_version (and stale per-item doc_versions via mapItemToPayload), which will fail or mis-merge if the backend enforces optimistic locking. The same pattern exists in the GRN form (use-grn-form-actions.ts onSubmit edit path sends stale item doc_versions; header doc_version is silently dropped because it is not in headerKeys).
confirmed.suggested_fix: On update success, `form.reset(getDefaultValues(freshData))` once the refetched entity arrives (e.g. useEffect keyed on creditNote.doc_version calling form.reset when in view mode), so subsequent edits carry the current doc_version values.

votes.reason: The finding is accurate: CnForm's update onSuccess only setMode("view") with no form.reset and no key-based remount in [id]/_content.tsx, so a second edit+save sends the mount-time doc_version (cn-form.tsx line 92) and stale item doc_versions via mapItemToPayload; in GRN, use-grn-form-actions.ts likewise never resets on success, sends stale item doc_versions via mapDetailToPayload, and silently drops the header doc_version because headerKeys (lines 129-148) excludes it from the patch payload.

Verified in code: after update success cn-form.tsx only calls setMode("view") with no form.reset and no remount key on CnForm, so a second edit+save in the same session reachably sends the pre-save doc_version (line 92) and stale item doc_versions via mapItemToPayload; the GRN edit path likewise omits doc_version from headerKeys and reuses stale item versions — the impact is real for any backend optimistic-lock check, which the pervasive doc_version plumbing implies exists.

Confirmed, not refuted: CnForm has no key/remount, no values: option, and no reset on update success (onSuccess only toasts + setMode("view") at cn-form.tsx:118-121), so a second consecutive save sends mount-time doc_version (line 92) and stale per-item doc_versions via mapItemToPayload (cn-form-schema.ts:228); the GRN edit path likewise sends stale item doc_versions and drops header doc_version because it is absent from headerKeys (use-grn-form-actions.ts:129-148).

low.description: Dynamically interpolated Tailwind classes `bg-${card.color}/10` and `text-${card.color}` cannot be detected by Tailwind v4's source scanner. The classes only work if the literal string happens to appear elsewhere in the codebase: `bg-primary/10`, `bg-info/10`, `bg-warning/10`, `text-info`, `text-warning` do exist in other files, but `bg-secondary/10` and standalone `text-secondary` appear nowhere literally (verified by grep), so the Store Requisition summary card's icon tint and color are silently unstyled.
low.suggested_fix: Replace the interpolation with a static lookup map, e.g. `const COLOR_CLASSES = { primary: "bg-primary/10 text-primary", info: "bg-info/10 text-info", warning: "bg-warning/10 text-warning", secondary: "bg-secondary/10 text-secondary" }` and store the full class strings in SUMMARY_CARDS so Tailwind can see them.

findings.description: Stale Next.js idiom survived the migration: resolveServerImageUrl() builds evidence-image URLs of the form /api/proxy/api/{bu}/documents/{id}/download (and rewrites relative '/api/...' fileUrls to '/api/proxy/api/...'). These URLs are consumed as raw in EntryNotesDialog/ImageThumb (used by pc-entry-notes-dialog.tsx:51 and sc-entry-notes-dialog.tsx:48). In the Next.js source app /api/proxy/[...path] was a real server route that proxied and attached auth; in this SPA the '/api/proxy/' prefix is only rewritten inside lib/http-client.ts fetches (PROXY_PREFIX, lib/http-client.ts:81), which an tag never goes through. Verified the deployment paths: S3/CloudFront static hosting has no /api route at all (404); the Vite dev proxy and docker/nginx.conf.template `location /api/` both forward the path verbatim, so the backend receives /api/proxy/api/... which it does not serve; and requests cannot carry the in-memory Bearer token, so even a correct path to an authenticated download endpoint would 401. Net effect: server-stored note evidence images render as broken thumbnails in every SPA deployment mode whenever the backend returns a relative fileUrl or only a fileToken (absolute fileUrls are the only case that still works).
findings.suggested_fix: Resolve the image URL against runtime config instead of the dead Next proxy: build `${BACKEND_URL}/api/{bu}/documents/{id}/download/...` from getRuntimeConfig(), and fetch the image via httpClient (which attaches Authorization/x-app-id) into a blob/object URL — e.g. a small useAuthedImage hook — or have the backend return presigned/absolute URLs. At minimum, stop prefixing '/api/proxy'.

findings.description: Missing i18n key: the save button renders tc("saving") while isSaving, but `common.saving` does not exist in messages/en.json or messages/th.json (verified programmatically; common has only `save`/`saveDraft`). use-intl will log an error and render the raw key path 'common.saving' on the button while a note is being saved. (Key is also absent in the source app — inherited bug, but live in this repo.)
findings.suggested_fix: Add `saving` to the `common` namespace in messages/en.json and messages/th.json, or switch to the existing `form.saving` key (used successfully via tform("saving") in sc-method-config.ts).

findings.description: Inconsistent counted semantics make the spot-check flow uncompletable for zero-stock items. isCountedFn (line 89) treats an item as counted only when eff > 0 (comment says 0 = not counted, deliberately). But handleSetUncountedToZero (line 146) sets all uncounted items to 0 — which still leaves them 'uncounted' under that rule, so clicking 'Set empty to zero' never decreases uncountedCount, the progress bar doesn't move, and the Submit-for-review button (gated on uncountedCount === 0 at line 422) never appears. The only observable effect is that those items get included with actual_qty 0 in the save payload. An item whose true count is 0 can never be confirmed, blocking submission. Physical count (pc-entry-component) uses eff != null instead, where the same button works as expected.
findings.suggested_fix: Either align isCountedFn with physical count (eff != null counts as counted, letting 0 be a valid count), or remove/disable the setEmptyToZero button for spot checks and surface the rule that every item must be counted with qty > 0.

findings.description: Cost probes overwrite persisted document values, including in view mode. ProductCell renders StockInCostProbe/StockOutCostProbe even when disabled (view mode, lines 143-150). StockInCostProbe's effect (lines 86-96) writes cost_per_unit/total_cost from the live cost API with shouldDirty: true, so opening an existing stock-in adjustment silently replaces its saved costs with the current average cost and marks those fields dirty — buildItemChanges in ia-form.tsx then treats untouched rows as edited on update, and TotalCostCell displays the recomputed value instead of the stored one. StockOutCostProbe (lines 112-117) is worse: it sets the line's total_cost to the product's *last receiving* total (qty-independent, per hooks/use-product-cost.ts useProductLastReceiving), clobbering the qty x cost recalcTotal whenever its query resolves. Behavior is inherited from the source app, but it makes saved totals unstable and qty-insensitive for stock-out lines.
findings.suggested_fix: Render the probes only when !disabled (i.e., add/edit), gate the setValue calls so they don't run for rows hydrated from an existing document (e.g., skip when the row has an id and the user hasn't changed qty/product), and for stock-out derive total_cost = qty * unit cost from the last-receiving unit price rather than copying its document total.

findings.description: Retry refetches the wrong query when a previous period is selected. error is derived as `previousPeriodId ? errorSelected : errorCurrent` (line 166), but `refetch` is destructured only from usePhysicalCountPeriodCurrent (line 152). When usePhysicalCountPeriodDetail fails, the ErrorState retry button refetches the *current* period query, so the failing selected-period query is never retried and the error screen persists.
findings.suggested_fix: Destructure refetch from usePhysicalCountPeriodDetail too and pick it the same way as error: `const refetch = previousPeriodId ? refetchSelected : refetchCurrent`.

findings.description: handleRefresh shows a success toast even when the refresh fails. TanStack Query's refetch() resolves with a result object and does not reject on query error, so `await refetch(); toast.success(t("productsRefreshed"))` always reports success and the catch/errorToast branch is dead code. Contrast with pc-entry-component's handleRefresh, which goes through a mutation's onError.
findings.suggested_fix: Inspect the refetch result: `const res = await refetch(); if (res.error) { errorToast(res.error); } else { toast.success(...); }`.

findings.description: Unhandled error path: usePeriodEndReview() destructures data/isLoading/isFetching/refetch but never the error. If the review fetch fails, isLoading goes false with no data, and the page renders only the header — no error message, no retry affordance (the Refresh button exists but nothing tells the user the load failed). Every sibling page in this zone (pc-component, sc-component, transaction-component, ia-component) renders <ErrorState> for this case.
findings.suggested_fix: Destructure error and render <ErrorState message={error.message} onRetry={() => refetch()} /> like the other inventory-management pages.

findings.description: Dead fetch / unfinished UI: PeHistory calls usePeriodEnd() and maps `items = data?.data ?? []`, but the render is `isLoading ? skeleton : items.length === 0 ? <EmptyComponent/> : null` — when history items exist, it renders null. The closed-period history list is never displayed, and the list query result is fetched but unused (wasted request on every period-end page visit).
findings.suggested_fix: Render the history items (e.g., a simple card/table of past periods), or drop the usePeriodEnd() call and the section until the feature is implemented.

confirmed.description: Stale Next.js idiom survived the migration: resolveServerImageUrl() builds evidence-image URLs of the form /api/proxy/api/{bu}/documents/{id}/download (and rewrites relative '/api/...' fileUrls to '/api/proxy/api/...'). These URLs are consumed as raw in EntryNotesDialog/ImageThumb (used by pc-entry-notes-dialog.tsx:51 and sc-entry-notes-dialog.tsx:48). In the Next.js source app /api/proxy/[...path] was a real server route that proxied and attached auth; in this SPA the '/api/proxy/' prefix is only rewritten inside lib/http-client.ts fetches (PROXY_PREFIX, lib/http-client.ts:81), which an tag never goes through. Verified the deployment paths: S3/CloudFront static hosting has no /api route at all (404); the Vite dev proxy and docker/nginx.conf.template `location /api/` both forward the path verbatim, so the backend receives /api/proxy/api/... which it does not serve; and requests cannot carry the in-memory Bearer token, so even a correct path to an authenticated download endpoint would 401. Net effect: server-stored note evidence images render as broken thumbnails in every SPA deployment mode whenever the backend returns a relative fileUrl or only a fileToken (absolute fileUrls are the only case that still works).
confirmed.suggested_fix: Resolve the image URL against runtime config instead of the dead Next proxy: build `${BACKEND_URL}/api/{bu}/documents/{id}/download/...` from getRuntimeConfig(), and fetch the image via httpClient (which attaches Authorization/x-app-id) into a blob/object URL — e.g. a small useAuthedImage hook — or have the backend return presigned/absolute URLs. At minimum, stop prefixing '/api/proxy'.

votes.reason: The finding is technically correct: resolveServerImageUrl (entry-notes-dialog.tsx:388-410) emits /api/proxy/... URLs that are fed straight into in ImageThumb (line 354), but the /api/proxy prefix is only stripped inside lib/http-client.ts fetches (PROXY_PREFIX, lines 81-94) — there is no service worker, the Vite dev proxy and docker/nginx.conf.template both forward /api/* verbatim without rewriting, S3/CloudFront static hosting has no /api route, and the Bearer token is in-memory-only so an cannot authenticate; hence server-stored evidence images with relative fileUrls or fileToken-only fail to load in every deployment mode (the only nitpick is they show a graceful icon fallback rather than a literal broken-image thumbnail).

The finding is correct and the impact is real: resolveServerImageUrl's /api/proxy-prefixed URLs are consumed as raw in ImageThumb, the /api/proxy rewrite exists only inside lib/http-client.ts fetches which never uses, all three deployment modes (S3 no /api route, Vite proxy and nginx proxy_pass both forwarding the path verbatim with the unserved /api/proxy prefix) fail to deliver the request, the codebase's own comment-sheet.tsx:145 confirms relative fileUrls are the backend's normal response shape, and with in-memory-only Bearer auth and no service worker an could not authenticate even with a corrected path.

Confirmed, not refuted: the /api/proxy-prefixed image URLs from resolveServerImageUrl (entry-notes-dialog.tsx:388) are used as raw which bypasses the only place the prefix is rewritten (lib/http-client.ts resolveUrl), and all three deployment paths (S3 no /api route, Vite proxy and nginx location /api/ both forwarding the path verbatim to a backend that doesn't serve /api/proxy/*) plus the in-memory-only Bearer token mean relative-fileUrl/fileToken evidence images cannot render in this SPA.

confirmed.description: Inconsistent counted semantics make the spot-check flow uncompletable for zero-stock items. isCountedFn (line 89) treats an item as counted only when eff > 0 (comment says 0 = not counted, deliberately). But handleSetUncountedToZero (line 146) sets all uncounted items to 0 — which still leaves them 'uncounted' under that rule, so clicking 'Set empty to zero' never decreases uncountedCount, the progress bar doesn't move, and the Submit-for-review button (gated on uncountedCount === 0 at line 422) never appears. The only observable effect is that those items get included with actual_qty 0 in the save payload. An item whose true count is 0 can never be confirmed, blocking submission. Physical count (pc-entry-component) uses eff != null instead, where the same button works as expected.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Either align isCountedFn with physical count (eff != null counts as counted, letting 0 be a valid count), or remove/disable the setEmptyToZero button for spot checks and surface the rule that every item must be counted with qty > 0.

votes.reason: The claim is accurate: isCountedFn (sc-entry-component.tsx:89-92) requires eff > 0, so handleSetUncountedToZero (line 146) setting uncounted items to 0 leaves them uncounted — uncountedCount never reaches 0, the Submit-for-review button gated at line 422 never appears, and the only effect is actual_qty 0 entries in the save/submit payloads (lines 249/268 use eff != null), whereas pc-entry-component.tsx (lines 104/417) counts eff != null so its identical button does work.

Confirmed, not refuted: isCountedFn (eff > 0, line 89-92) makes handleSetUncountedToZero (line 146) a provable no-op for progress since it writes 0 which stays "uncounted", and grep shows useReviewSpotCheck is only used in sc-entry-component behind the uncountedCount === 0 gate (line 422), so a zero-stock item genuinely blocks submission with no alternate path — pc-entry-component's eff != null semantics confirm the inconsistency.

Confirmed by code trace: handleSetUncountedToZero sets counts to 0 but isCountedFn requires eff > 0 (and actual_qty is non-nullable number, defaulting 0), so uncountedCount/progress never change and the Submit-for-review gate (uncountedCount === 0, line 422) is unreachable for any zero-stock item — though note this is a faithful port of identical logic in the source Next.js app, not a migration regression.

confirmed.description: Cost probes overwrite persisted document values, including in view mode. ProductCell renders StockInCostProbe/StockOutCostProbe even when disabled (view mode, lines 143-150). StockInCostProbe's effect (lines 86-96) writes cost_per_unit/total_cost from the live cost API with shouldDirty: true, so opening an existing stock-in adjustment silently replaces its saved costs with the current average cost and marks those fields dirty — buildItemChanges in ia-form.tsx then treats untouched rows as edited on update, and TotalCostCell displays the recomputed value instead of the stored one. StockOutCostProbe (lines 112-117) is worse: it sets the line's total_cost to the product's *last receiving* total (qty-independent, per hooks/use-product-cost.ts useProductLastReceiving), clobbering the qty x cost recalcTotal whenever its query resolves. Behavior is inherited from the source app, but it makes saved totals unstable and qty-insensitive for stock-out lines.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Render the probes only when !disabled (i.e., add/edit), gate the setValue calls so they don't run for rows hydrated from an existing document (e.g., skip when the row has an id and the user hasn't changed qty/product), and for stock-out derive total_cost = qty * unit cost from the last-receiving unit price rather than copying its document total.

votes.reason: The claim is accurate: ProductCell's disabled branch (ia-item-table.tsx:143-150) does render the cost probes in view mode; StockInCostProbe's effect (lines 86-96) overwrites hydrated cost_per_unit/total_cost with live API values using shouldDirty:true; switching to edit (ia-form.tsx:228) never resets the form so buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252) treats those rows as updated; TotalCostCell watches the form value so it shows the recomputed total; and StockOutCostProbe (lines 112-117) copies the last receiving's document total_cost (which is qty-independent per hooks/use-product-cost.ts) over the qty*cost recalcTotal result — the only minor caveat is that shouldDirty won't flag dirty when the live value equals the saved one, which doesn't undermine the finding.

Not refutable: the probes demonstrably render in view mode (ia-item-table.tsx lines 143-150) with their queries enabled by document-hydrated location/product/qty values against real endpoints, their effects overwrite cost_per_unit/total_cost with shouldDirty:true, the view→edit transition does no form.reset so buildItemChanges (lib/form-helpers.ts:249-252) includes untouched rows in the update payload with live-API costs, and StockOutCostProbe copies the last GRN's qty-independent document total over the qty×cost recalculation — every step of the claimed impact is reachable for any editable (draft) document.

low.description: Missing i18n key: the save button renders tc("saving") while isSaving, but `common.saving` does not exist in messages/en.json or messages/th.json (verified programmatically; common has only `save`/`saveDraft`). use-intl will log an error and render the raw key path 'common.saving' on the button while a note is being saved. (Key is also absent in the source app — inherited bug, but live in this repo.)

low.description: Retry refetches the wrong query when a previous period is selected. error is derived as `previousPeriodId ? errorSelected : errorCurrent` (line 166), but `refetch` is destructured only from usePhysicalCountPeriodCurrent (line 152). When usePhysicalCountPeriodDetail fails, the ErrorState retry button refetches the *current* period query, so the failing selected-period query is never retried and the error screen persists.
low.suggested_fix: Destructure refetch from usePhysicalCountPeriodDetail too and pick it the same way as error: `const refetch = previousPeriodId ? refetchSelected : refetchCurrent`.

low.description: handleRefresh shows a success toast even when the refresh fails. TanStack Query's refetch() resolves with a result object and does not reject on query error, so `await refetch(); toast.success(t("productsRefreshed"))` always reports success and the catch/errorToast branch is dead code. Contrast with pc-entry-component's handleRefresh, which goes through a mutation's onError.
low.suggested_fix: Inspect the refetch result: `const res = await refetch(); if (res.error) { errorToast(res.error); } else { toast.success(...); }`.

low.description: Unhandled error path: usePeriodEndReview() destructures data/isLoading/isFetching/refetch but never the error. If the review fetch fails, isLoading goes false with no data, and the page renders only the header — no error message, no retry affordance (the Refresh button exists but nothing tells the user the load failed). Every sibling page in this zone (pc-component, sc-component, transaction-component, ia-component) renders <ErrorState> for this case.
low.suggested_fix: Destructure error and render <ErrorState message={error.message} onRetry={() => refetch()} /> like the other inventory-management pages.

low.description: Dead fetch / unfinished UI: PeHistory calls usePeriodEnd() and maps `items = data?.data ?? []`, but the render is `isLoading ? skeleton : items.length === 0 ? <EmptyComponent/> : null` — when history items exist, it renders null. The closed-period history list is never displayed, and the list query result is fetched but unused (wasted request on every period-end page visit).
low.suggested_fix: Render the history items (e.g., a simple card/table of past periods), or drop the usePeriodEnd() call and the section until the feature is implemented.

low.description: Hardcoded English UI strings bypass i18n in the transaction module: the mobile filter sheet button literal "Done" (line 407 — tc("done") exists and is used for the identical button in ia-component.tsx:414), the "Qty In"/"Qty Out" column headers in use-transaction-table.tsx (lines 138, 156), and the inline "parsing…" status text in _shared/entry-import-dialog.tsx (line 266). Thai-locale users see English for these.

findings.description: Desktop list path computes totalRecords as items.length (current page size) instead of the server total: `const totalRecords = useInfiniteScroll ? grid.totalRecords : items.length;`. Every sibling system-admin module uses `data?.paginate?.total ?? 0` (user, workflow, period, running-code, activity-log, document, user-activity). Consequences: useRoleTable pageCount = ceil(items.length/perpage) is always 1, so DataGridPagination can never navigate past page 1 when there are more roles than one page, and the header badge shows the page row count, not the total. (Bug also exists in the source Next app, so it survived migration unfixed.)
findings.suggested_fix: Change to `const totalRecords = useInfiniteScroll ? grid.totalRecords : (data?.paginate?.total ?? 0);` matching the other modules.

findings.description: Missing i18n key: `tfl("field")` resolves to `field.field`, which does not exist in messages/en.json nor messages/th.json (verified programmatically — this is the only missing key in the whole zone). use-intl will log an error and render the raw fallback for the condition-field label in the workflow routing rule editor.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"field": "Field"` to the `field` namespace in messages/en.json and `"field": "ฟิลด์"` in messages/th.json (or reuse an existing key such as a dedicated systemAdmin.workflow key).

findings.description: On mobile/infinite-scroll, fetch errors are silently swallowed across all system-admin list pages. The desktop query is disabled (`enabled: !useInfiniteScroll`) so `error` is always null on mobile, and `useGridPagination` (hooks/use-grid-pagination.ts) does not expose an error at all — it returns only items/isLoading/hasMore. A failed list request on mobile renders EmptyComponent ("no data") with no retry, instead of ErrorState. This affects role, user, workflow, period, document, running-code, activity-log and user-activity components identically.
findings.suggested_fix: Surface `error`/`refetch` from the underlying query in useGridPagination and render ErrorState in the mobile branch of each list component (or at least in the shared hook consumers).

findings.description: Duplicate error toast: `runMutation` calls `toast.error(err.message)` in onError and then rejects; the caller `handle()` catches the same error and calls `toast.error(err.message)` again. Toggling a workflow active or duplicating one that fails shows the same error toast twice.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the toast from runMutation's onError (keep the reject), or in handle() skip toasting errors that already came from runMutation (e.g., rethrow a flagged error).

findings.description: `useUser()` and `useProduct()` are called with no params, so the request goes out without page/perpage and the backend applies its default pagination. The workflow detail's stage user-assignment picker and product list therefore only see the first page of users/products, silently hiding the rest. (Same code exists in the source app; contrast with user-assigned-form.tsx which deliberately passes `{ perpage: 999 }` for locations.)
findings.suggested_fix: Pass `{ perpage: -1 }` (the convention used by usePermission/useDepartment callers in this repo) or an explicit large perpage to both hooks, or switch to a search-as-you-type lookup endpoint.

findings.description: Two related issues: (1) `useRole()` is called without params, so the roles checklist for assignment only contains the backend's default first page of roles. (2) The useEffect syncing `locationTargetKeys` from `initialLocationKeys` depends on an array recreated each render (`userLocations.map(...)`) and resets any locally-edited target keys back to server state whenever the userLocations query data changes (e.g., invalidation after the roles mutation settles while the locations mutation is still pending, or any background refetch) — wiping unsaved Transfer selections. It currently behaves only because babel-plugin-react-compiler memoizes the derived array and refetchOnWindowFocus is disabled; the code is correct-by-accident.
findings.suggested_fix: Call useRole with `{ perpage: -1 }`. For the sync effect, key it on a stable serialization (e.g., joined sorted ids) and only reset target keys when not in edit mode (`isView`).

findings.description: `monaco.languages.registerCompletionItemProvider("sql", ...)` is called in onMount with no disposal. Monaco is a global singleton that survives route changes, so each visit to /system-admin/query-dataset registers another provider — completion suggestions duplicate (2x, 3x...) after navigating away and back, and old closures are leaked.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep the IDisposable returned by registerCompletionItemProvider in a ref and dispose it in a useEffect cleanup on unmount (or register once behind a module-level guard).

findings.description: The file-type filter is applied client-side to only the currently loaded page (`allDocuments.filter(...)`) while pagination remains server-driven. With a type filter active, totalRecords becomes documents.length of the current page, so pagination collapses to one page and matching documents on later server pages are unreachable/invisible. Misleading counts and incomplete results when more than one page of documents exists.
findings.suggested_fix: Send the type filter to the backend as a query param (contentType filter) so pagination and totals stay consistent, or fetch all pages before filtering client-side.

findings.description: The SMTP password is returned by `GET app-config key=report_email`, stored in react-query cache and reset into a form field (`smtp_password`), then echoed back on save. The secret round-trips through the browser in plaintext on every page view (visible in devtools/network/React state). No token-persistence violation (access token stays in memory), but secret-handling here relies entirely on the backend exposing the raw password.
findings.suggested_fix: Backend-side: return a masked placeholder for smtp.password and only update it when a new value is submitted. Frontend: treat empty password as "unchanged" instead of requiring it (schema currently demands min(1), forcing the plaintext round-trip).

findings.description: Dead code: `PermissionMatrix` is exported but never imported anywhere in the repo (role-form uses PermissionPicker instead). Verified via repo-wide grep.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete routes/system-admin/role/_components/permission-matrix.tsx, or wire it up if it was meant to replace PermissionPicker.

findings.description: Dead code: the 680-line `SignatureConfig` component (drag-and-drop signature editor with workflow import) is not imported by any route or component in this repo (verified repo-wide grep excluding itself; it is also unused in the source Next app). It silently rots and its hooks (useSignatureCandidates, useWorkflowTypeQuery usage patterns) are untested by any live page.
findings.suggested_fix: Either register the intended document-config pages that consume it, or delete the file until that feature is ported.

findings.description: `data?.paginate.total ?? 0` chains optionally on `data` but not on `paginate`. Every other system-admin component uses `data?.paginate?.total ?? 0`. If the endpoint ever responds without a paginate envelope (error-shaped 200, older gateway), this throws a TypeError and takes down the page instead of degrading.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `data?.paginate?.total ?? 0` for consistency with the rest of the zone.

Confirmed live against the local gateway: GET users/products with no params returns only the backend-default first page (10 of 11 users, 10 of 2593 products, perpage=10), and wf-stage-users.tsx/wf-products.tsx filter only the passed-in prop arrays client-side, so the workflow detail picker genuinely hides all rows beyond page 1 — the finding is real, not theoretical.

votes.reason: Both failure modes are unreal: the backend's application-roles endpoint defaults to perpage 100 (role_permission.service.ts: `p.perpage ?? 100`), so useRole() without params returns effectively all roles, and the claimed wipe trigger cannot happen because useUpdateUserRoles invalidates ["users"] which is disjoint from the ["user-locations", bu, userId] key, while refetchOnWindowFocus is globally false, react-query structural sharing keeps the data reference stable, the effect has a sorted content-equality guard, and the react-compiler memoization is an unconditional build-wide setting (vite.config.ts) rather than an accident — the reviewer's finding itself concedes the code currently behaves correctly.

Both impacts are theoretical: the backend's application-roles endpoint (role_permission.service.ts) defaults to perpage ?? 100, so useRole() without params returns the full realistic role catalog (and matches the source app); and the sync effect cannot wipe edits in practice because the roles mutation invalidates QUERY_KEYS.USERS ("users") which does not overlap the ["user-locations", ...] query key, the only user-locations invalidation fires after a successful locations save when server keys equal local keys (absorbed by the effect's set-equality guard, followed by navigation away), and refetchOnWindowFocus:false plus the project-wide react-compiler are deliberate configuration rather than accidents — leaving no reachable trigger for the claimed data loss.

Neither issue manifests at runtime: the backend defaults application-roles to perpage 100 (role.service.ts `p.perpage ?? 100`), so `useRole()` returns all roles unless a BU exceeds 100; and the claimed wipe scenario is impossible because the roles mutation invalidates `["users"]` which does not match the `["user-locations", ...]` query key, the locations mutation's own invalidation only refires after save (when server keys equal local keys, so the effect's sorted-equality guard keeps `prev`) followed by immediate navigation, refetchOnWindowFocus is disabled, and the react-compiler memoization the code relies on is unconditionally part of the build in vite.config.ts — making "correct-by-accident" a robustness nit, not a reproducible bug.

low.description: Duplicate error toast: `runMutation` calls `toast.error(err.message)` in onError and then rejects; the caller `handle()` catches the same error and calls `toast.error(err.message)` again. Toggling a workflow active or duplicating one that fails shows the same error toast twice.
low.suggested_fix: Remove the toast from runMutation's onError (keep the reject), or in handle() skip toasting errors that already came from runMutation (e.g., rethrow a flagged error).

low.description: `monaco.languages.registerCompletionItemProvider("sql", ...)` is called in onMount with no disposal. Monaco is a global singleton that survives route changes, so each visit to /system-admin/query-dataset registers another provider — completion suggestions duplicate (2x, 3x...) after navigating away and back, and old closures are leaked.
low.suggested_fix: Keep the IDisposable returned by registerCompletionItemProvider in a ref and dispose it in a useEffect cleanup on unmount (or register once behind a module-level guard).

low.description: The file-type filter is applied client-side to only the currently loaded page (`allDocuments.filter(...)`) while pagination remains server-driven. With a type filter active, totalRecords becomes documents.length of the current page, so pagination collapses to one page and matching documents on later server pages are unreachable/invisible. Misleading counts and incomplete results when more than one page of documents exists.
low.suggested_fix: Send the type filter to the backend as a query param (contentType filter) so pagination and totals stay consistent, or fetch all pages before filtering client-side.

low.description: The SMTP password is returned by `GET app-config key=report_email`, stored in react-query cache and reset into a form field (`smtp_password`), then echoed back on save. The secret round-trips through the browser in plaintext on every page view (visible in devtools/network/React state). No token-persistence violation (access token stays in memory), but secret-handling here relies entirely on the backend exposing the raw password.
low.suggested_fix: Backend-side: return a masked placeholder for smtp.password and only update it when a new value is submitted. Frontend: treat empty password as "unchanged" instead of requiring it (schema currently demands min(1), forcing the plaintext round-trip).

low.description: Dead code: `PermissionMatrix` is exported but never imported anywhere in the repo (role-form uses PermissionPicker instead). Verified via repo-wide grep.
low.suggested_fix: Delete routes/system-admin/role/_components/permission-matrix.tsx, or wire it up if it was meant to replace PermissionPicker.

low.description: Dead code: the 680-line `SignatureConfig` component (drag-and-drop signature editor with workflow import) is not imported by any route or component in this repo (verified repo-wide grep excluding itself; it is also unused in the source Next app). It silently rots and its hooks (useSignatureCandidates, useWorkflowTypeQuery usage patterns) are untested by any live page.
low.suggested_fix: Either register the intended document-config pages that consume it, or delete the file until that feature is ported.

low.description: `data?.paginate.total ?? 0` chains optionally on `data` but not on `paginate`. Every other system-admin component uses `data?.paginate?.total ?? 0`. If the endpoint ever responds without a paginate envelope (error-shaped 200, older gateway), this throws a TypeError and takes down the page instead of degrading.
low.suggested_fix: Use `data?.paginate?.total ?? 0` for consistency with the rest of the zone.

findings.description: Thai address data files are missing from the repo. vendor-address.tsx fetches /data/thai-subdistricts.json, /data/thai-districts.json and /data/thai-provinces.json (also via hooks/use-thai-address.ts and the LookupThai* components), but public/ contains only config.json — the source app's public/data/thai-{provinces,districts,subdistricts}.json were never copied during the Next->Vite migration and no build script copies them. In an SPA, these URLs hit the index.html fallback, so res.json()/r.json() throws. Result: postal-code autofill and the province/district/subdistrict dropdowns in the vendor address form are completely broken, with unhandled promise rejections on every postal-code keystroke.
findings.suggested_fix: Copy ../carmen-inventory-frontend/public/data/thai-provinces.json, thai-districts.json and thai-subdistricts.json into public/data/ in this repo.

findings.description: resolveFromPostalCode rejections are unhandled: the mount useEffect calls resolveFromPostalCode(postalCode).then(...) with no .catch, and handlePostalCodeChange awaits it inside an async event handler with no try/catch. Any fetch/JSON failure (missing files, offline) surfaces as an unhandled promise rejection instead of degrading gracefully.
findings.suggested_fix: Add .catch(() => {}) (or try/catch with a silent fallback to manual entry) around resolveFromPostalCode calls in the useEffect and in handlePostalCodeChange.

findings.description: Leftover debug logging marked '🐛 DEBUG' in the submit error handler: console.warn("[vendor-form] validation failed:", errors) and console.warn("[vendor-form] values:", form.getValues()) dump the full form state (vendor contacts' emails/phones) to the console in production on every failed validation.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove both console.warn lines, keeping only scrollToFirstInvalidField().

findings.description: After a successful edit-save, form.reset(values) keeps vendors.add / vendors.remove populated. The update mutation invalidates the REQUEST_PRICE_LISTS key prefix (useApiMutation onSettled), which refetches useRequestPriceListById, so requestPriceList.vendors now includes the just-added vendors. displayVendors = existingVendors (from the refreshed prop) + addedVendors (still in form state) then renders each newly added vendor twice in view mode, and pressing Edit -> Save again re-sends the same vendors in vendors.add, creating duplicates server-side.
findings.suggested_fix: On update success reset with cleared deltas: form.reset({ ...values, vendors: { add: [], remove: [] } }) (or re-derive defaults from the refetched RFP via getDefaultValues).

findings.description: After a successful update, form.reset(values) keeps newly added address/contact rows in form state without ids (the byId refetch updates the vendor prop, but the form is never re-initialized from it). If the user clicks Edit again and saves, buildNestedPayload classifies those rows as add (filter !item.id) and re-sends them, duplicating addresses/contacts on the backend.
findings.suggested_fix: After update success, re-initialize the form from the refetched vendor (e.g., useEffect that calls form.reset(getDefaultValues(vendor)) while in view mode), instead of form.reset(values).

findings.description: Same stale-reset pattern as vendor-form/rfp-form: on update success form.reset(values) keeps new pricelist_detail rows without ids while the byId refetch gives those rows server ids in the priceList prop. On the next edit-save, submitUpdate's buildItemChanges compares form values against freshly recomputed defaultValues (which now include the server rows): the id-less form rows go into add again, and the server-created rows (ids present in defaults but absent from form values) go into remove — so a second save deletes and recreates detail rows (id churn) or duplicates them depending on backend behavior.
findings.suggested_fix: Re-initialize the form from the refetched priceList after a successful update (form.reset(getDefaultValues(priceList, ...)) once the query settles) rather than form.reset(values).

findings.description: Template edit only ever sends products: { add: <entire flattened list> } when details are dirty, and sends products: {} when the user has removed every product (products.length === 0 makes shouldSendProducts false). There is no update/remove channel (the detail schema has no id), so deleting all products is silently ignored, and if the backend treats 'add' as append (as the vendor/price-list endpoints do), editing any tier duplicates every existing product on the template.
findings.suggested_fix: Track detail ids in the PLT form schema and build an add/update/remove payload (mirroring buildItemChanges used by pl-form), or confirm and document that the backend replaces all products when 'add' is sent and explicitly send an empty add/remove when the list is cleared.

findings.description: ProductCell excludes every product_id selected in other rows from LookupProduct. But multi-tier templates are a legitimate state: plt-form-schema getDefaultValues flattens product.moq into multiple rows with the same product_id, and groupDetailsToProducts re-groups them on submit. For a loaded multi-tier template, each duplicate row's own selected product is in its excludeIds, so LookupProduct filters it from the items list and cannot resolve the selected label (shows placeholder); the user also cannot add a second MOQ tier for an existing product.
findings.suggested_fix: Either drop the exclusion (duplicates are valid for MOQ tiers) or exclude by (product_id, unit_id) pair; at minimum keep the row's own current value resolvable by not filtering an id that equals the row's selected value.

findings.description: Grid-view VendorCard reads the primary contact only from item.tb_vendor_contact (marked @deprecated in types/vendor.ts: 'use contacts'). Everywhere else in the zone falls back across field names (rfp-form uses contacts ?? tb_vendor_contact; vendor-form-schema uses vendor_contact ?? contacts ?? tb_vendor_contact). If the list API returns 'contacts', the phone/email block silently never renders in the card.
findings.suggested_fix: const contacts = item.contacts ?? item.tb_vendor_contact ?? []; const primaryContact = contacts.find((c) => c.is_primary);

findings.description: Template literal missing a space: `h-7 text-xs${ ... ? "ring-destructive ring-1" : ""}` produces the class string 'h-7 text-xsring-destructive ring-1' when the city field has an error — both text-xs and ring-destructive are corrupted, so the intended red ring (and text size) is not applied. Also inconsistent with the cn() utility used everywhere else.
findings.suggested_fix: Use cn("h-7 text-xs", form.formState.errors.vendor_address?.[index]?.city && "ring-destructive ring-1").

findings.description: Dead code surviving the migration: five files in this zone are not imported by anything (verified by symbol and path grep across the repo): price-list/_components/pl-detail-view.tsx, price-list/_components/use-pl-detail-table.tsx, price-list-template/_components/plt-detail-view.tsx, price-list-template/_components/use-plt-detail-table.tsx, and price-list-template/_components/plt-product-card.tsx (only referenced in a comment in rfp-vendor-section.tsx). findings.suggested_fix: Delete the five unreferenced files (and confirm with bunx tsc --noEmit && bun test:run).

findings.description: Stale Next.js-era JSDoc throughout the zone's page/_content files: comments still describe components as 'Server Component', reference 'Next.js App Router' routing and 'unwrap params with use() / params Promise' (e.g., vendor/page.tsx, vendor/new/page.tsx, vendor/[id]/_content.tsx, price-list-template/[id]/_content.tsx, price-list/new/page.tsx, request-price-list/new/page.tsx).
