findings.suggested_fix: Update the JSDoc blocks to describe React Router routes (useParams-based id pages, plain client components) or trim them to one-line descriptions.

findings.description: handleCopyUrl calls navigator.clipboard.writeText(url) without awaiting or handling rejection, then unconditionally sets copied=true — on clipboard permission failure (non-secure context, denied permission) the UI shows the success check mark even though nothing was copied, plus an unhandled rejection.
findings.suggested_fix: await/then the writeText promise and only setCopied(true) on success; show a toast on failure.

Not refutable: useUpdatePriceList's onSettled invalidation prefix-matches the mounted byId query, which refetches and re-renders the still-mounted PriceListForm with server-id detail rows in recomputed defaultValues, while form.reset(values) at pl-form.tsx:151 keeps the id-less rows in form state — so a second edit-save's buildItemChanges (add = !item.id rows, remove = default ids missing from form) really does emit add+remove for the same rows.

The finding holds: excludeIds is index-based so a duplicate row's own product_id (present at another index) is excluded, LookupCombobox then cannot resolve the label (LookupProduct passes no defaultLabel) and shows the placeholder; multi-tier templates are reachable real data because the backend persists moq arrays unvalidated (order_unit_obj, z.any()) and the source app's form used PltProductCard.handleAddTier (appending duplicate product_id rows) in production until commit be1885e2 (2026-05-05), so legacy templates with moq.length>=2 exist and getDefaultValues flattens them into exactly the broken state.

The finding manifests: plt-form.tsx:82 calls getDefaultValues which flattens product.moq into multiple details rows sharing one product_id; ProductCell's index-based filter (plt-product-table.tsx:222-225) still puts that shared id into excludeIds via the sibling row's index, LookupProduct removes it from items (lookup-product.tsx:65), and LookupCombobox resolves the selected label only via items.find with no defaultLabel fallback (lookup-combobox.tsx:157-167), so every duplicate-tier row in edit mode shows the placeholder instead of the product and the product cannot be picked for a new tier row.

findings.description: After a successful CREATE, EquipmentForm stays on /operation-plan/equipment/new and only does resetImage() + setMode("view") — it never navigates away, never stores the created id, and never resets the form. The toolbar then shows an Edit button (eq-toolbar.tsx line 100); clicking Edit and Save re-enters onSubmit where `isEdit && equipment` is false (the `equipment` prop is undefined on the new page), so the else-branch runs createEquipment.mutate again and creates a DUPLICATE record. findings.description: getDefaultValues() hardcodes `info: ""` and `dimension: ""` even when the loaded recipe has data (types/recipe.ts declares both as `Record<string, unknown> | null`), and there is no UI field bound to either. buildRecipePayload (recipe-form.tsx lines 253-254) always sends `info: textToObject("") => null` and `dimension: null` in the PATCH body (hooks/use-recipe.ts serializes the full data object into the multipart `data` part). So editing and saving any recipe sends explicit nulls that will wipe existing info/dimension on the server if the backend applies them (it applies explicit nulls for other optional fields like description in this same payload contract).
findings.suggested_fix: Either round-trip the values — `info: recipe.info ? JSON.stringify(recipe.info) : ""` (same for dimension) in getDefaultValues — or omit info/dimension from buildRecipePayload entirely since they are not editable in the form.

findings.description: The useEffect dependency array is `[computed, form]`, but `computed` is a fresh object literal built on every render, so the dependency never bails out: the effect runs after every single render of the recipe form and issues 7 form.setValue calls each time (every keystroke in any field). It works only because setValue with unchanged primitives doesn't loop, but the dep array is effectively a no-op and the redundant setValue churn runs on each render of a large form.
findings.suggested_fix: Depend on the individual primitive values instead: `[computed.costPerPortion, computed.grossMargin, computed.grossMarginPct, computed.actualFoodCostPct, computed.laborCostPct, computed.overheadPct, computed.suggestedPrice, form]`, or wrap `computed` in useMemo keyed on the watched inputs.

findings.description: `const previewUrl = file ? URL.createObjectURL(file) : null;` creates a new blob object URL during every render (a render-phase side effect). The cleanup effect on line 52 only revokes URLs from committed renders, so URLs created in renders React discards (StrictMode double-invoke, concurrent rendering) leak; and every re-render while a file is selected allocates+revokes a blob URL needlessly. Contrast with use-recipe-gallery.ts which correctly creates object URLs once per file inside an event handler and tracks them in a ref.
findings.suggested_fix: Memoize the URL per file: `const previewUrl = useMemo(() => (file ? URL.createObjectURL(file) : null), [file]);` keeping the existing revoke effect keyed on previewUrl.

findings.description: Not-found messages are hardcoded English strings that bypass i18n: "Equipment not found" here, "Cuisine not found" in cuisine/[id]/_content.tsx line 20, and "Recipe category not found" in category/[id]/_content.tsx line 26. The recipe equivalent (recipe/[id]/_content.tsx) correctly uses t("notFound") from the operationPlan.recipe namespace, so Thai users get an untranslated message on three of the four detail routes.
findings.suggested_fix: Add notFound keys under operationPlan.equipment / operationPlan.cuisine / operationPlan.recipeCategory in messages/{en,th}.json and use useTranslations(...)("notFound") like the recipe content component does.

findings.description: `move()` (and `remove()` at line 139-141) mix closure state with functional updates: the index/target is computed from the `items` snapshot captured at render time, but the swap is applied to `prev` inside setItems. If `prev` differs from the captured `items` (two updates batched before re-render), the swap is applied at stale indices, reordering the wrong slots. In practice each call comes from a separate click event so flushes interleave, but the pattern is fragile and inconsistent within the same file.
findings.suggested_fix: Compute the index inside the functional updater: `setItems(prev => { const idx = prev.findIndex(i => i.key === key); const to = idx + direction; if (idx < 0 || to < 0 || to >= prev.length) return prev; const next = [...prev]; [next[idx], next[to]] = [next[to], next[idx]]; return next; })`, and similarly look up the blob target inside remove's updater.

findings.description: useMyPendingStoreRequisition (the DEFAULT view of the SR list page in routes/store-operation/store-requisition) reads `json.data[0].data ?? []` without guarding `json.data[0]` — if the gateway returns `data: []` (user has no pending docs), this throws TypeError, the query errors, and the page renders ErrorState instead of an empty list. The sibling useStoreRequisition correctly guards with `json.data?.[0]`. It also reads `json.paginate` while useStoreRequisition reads `entry.paginate` (json.data[0].paginate); if the envelope nests paginate per-entry, my-pending totalRecords/pageCount are always 0 and the pagination badge/pager break in the default view.
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the useStoreRequisition parsing: `const entry = json.data?.[0]; return { data: entry?.data ?? [], paginate: entry?.paginate ?? {...} }` (verify actual envelope shape against the gateway once).

findings.description: On desktop both list queries are always enabled (`useMyPendingStoreRequisition` and `useStoreRequisition` both get `enabled: !useInfiniteScroll`) although only the one matching `viewMode` is rendered. Every search/filter/page change fires two network requests to two endpoints; the unused one is pure waste. (Inherited from the source app, but still a real cost.)
findings.suggested_fix: Gate each query on viewMode: `useMyPendingStoreRequisition(queryParams, { enabled: !useInfiniteScroll && viewMode === "my-pending" })` and the inverse for the all-document query.

findings.description: Mobile infinite scroll mixes the two document lists. `activeListHook` (my-pending vs all-document) is swapped into useGridPagination when viewMode changes (reachable on mobile via the filter Sheet buttons), but useGridPagination only resets its accumulated `allItems`/`page` when `params` change (`paramsKey` at hooks/use-grid-pagination.ts:45). queryParams are identical for both views, so after scrolling to page N and toggling viewMode, items already accumulated from the previous view remain in the list, the new view's page N is appended onto them, and pages 1..N-1 of the new view are never fetched.
findings.suggested_fix: Include viewMode in the reset key — e.g. pass a `resetKey` option to useGridPagination (or fold viewMode into params used for paramsKey) so allItems/page reset to 1 when the list hook changes.

[]` where `??` does not guard `json.data[0]` (TypeError on empty `data: []`), the sibling useStoreRequisition and the export hook both defensively guard `json.data?.[0]` for the same envelope, line 74 reads `json.paginate` while the sibling reads per-entry paginate (so my-pending totals fall back to 0 if nested), and sr-component.tsx confirms my-pending is the default viewMode whose query error renders ErrorState and whose paginate.total drives the badge/pager.

Backend trace (carmen-turborepo-backend-v2: micro-business store-requisition.service.ts findAllMyPending lines 1534-1718, gateway my-pending.store-requisition.service.ts:121, StdResponse.successPaginated) shows the my-pending envelope is { data: [{bu_code, bu_name, data: [...], total}], paginate: {grand} } — the per-BU entry is pushed unconditionally even with zero pending docs (data: [], total: 0) and the frontend always sends bu_code, so json.data is never [] on the claimed empty-list path and json.data[0].data does not throw; furthermore paginate lives at the top level for this endpoint (per-BU entries have only `total`, no `paginate`), so json.paginate is the correct read and the suggested fix (entry?.paginate) would itself zero out pagination — the asymmetry vs useStoreRequisition mirrors a real backend envelope asymmetry.

Traced to the actual gateway/micro-business code (carmen-turborepo-backend-v2 store-requisition.service.ts findAllMyPending + StdResponse.fromResult): when a user has no pending docs the endpoint still returns data:[{bu_code, bu_name, data: [], total: 0}] (a per-BU entry is always pushed for every resolved bu_code, and the hook only runs with a valid buCode), so json.data[0].data ?? [] yields [] and renders an empty list — no TypeError; and paginate is emitted at the top level of the envelope (not per-entry, entries carry `total` not `paginate`), so json.paginate is the correct read and the suggested entry?.paginate fix would actually break the working pagination.

low.description: LossValueCell contains a useEffect that does `form.setValue(`items.${index}.unit_cost`, unitCost)` — writing the watched value back to the same field it was read from, a pure no-op (dead leftover, presumably from when a loss_value field existed). It runs on every qty/unit_cost keystroke for every row.
low.suggested_fix: Delete the useEffect (lines 153-155); the computed `lossValue` display does not need it.

low.description: Index misalignment in active-filter removal: `selectedStatusLabels = selectedStatuses.map(...).filter(Boolean)` drops entries whose status key has no matching option, but the badge loop then pairs `selectedStatusLabels[i]` with `selectedStatuses[i]` and `removeStatusAt(i)`. If the URL contains an unknown doc_status value (hand-edited/stale link), labels and keys shift and clicking a badge's X removes the wrong status.
low.suggested_fix: Build label+key pairs in one pass (map to `{key, label}` then filter), and have removeStatusAt remove by key instead of positional index.

low.description: i18n inconsistency: the mobile/grid card status badge renders `config.label` from SR_STATUS_CONFIG, which createStatusConfig auto-generates as static English ('DRAFT', 'IN PROGRESS', ...), while the desktop table (use-sr-table.tsx:140) and header (sr-header.tsx:89) render the localized `ts(status)`. Thai users see untranslated statuses on cards. Same pattern in sr-workflow-history.tsx:55 (`config.label` for wf_* actions) and in the status filter options (STORE_REQUISITION_STATUS_OPTIONS labels).
low.suggested_fix: In SrCard use `useTranslations("status")` and render `ts(item.doc_status)` like the table does; consider the same for SrWorkflowHistory action labels.

low.description: Desktop grid display mode has no pagination: when the user toggles to grid view on desktop (isGridMode true, useInfiniteScroll false), SrCardList renders only the current page (`data?.data`) and neither DataGridPagination nor the infinite-scroll sentinel is rendered, so records beyond page 1 are unreachable in that mode. (Inherited from source app.)
low.suggested_fix: Render DataGridPagination (or a load-more control) under SrCardList when `isGridMode && !useInfiniteScroll`.

low.description: Dead alias: `const fromLocationLabelMap = locationLabelMap;` — both from/to badges read the same map; the alias adds nothing.
low.suggested_fix: Delete the alias and use `locationLabelMap` directly at line 292.

low.description: The profile-defaults useEffect re-runs whenever any dep changes (profile/defaultBu object identity changes on every refetch, plus `t`), and unconditionally fires `toast.warning(t("noDepartment"))` while `isAdd && !hasDepartment` — duplicate warning toasts can stack on profile refetches in add mode.
low.suggested_fix: Guard the toast with a `useRef` warned-once flag (or move it to a one-time check keyed on profile load completion).

findings.description: useEffect calls form.reset(defaultValues) with deps [defaultValues, form], but defaultValues is rebuilt by an IIFE on every render (new object identity), so the effect fires after EVERY re-render of CategoryForm. The component subscribes to form.formState.errors (lines 150, 163, 182), so a failed validation submit triggers a re-render, which immediately re-runs the effect and resets the form back to defaults — wiping all user input AND clearing the just-shown error messages. The same happens when isPending toggles after a failed mutation (server error): the user's entered data is silently discarded. (Bug exists identically in the source Next.js app — faithfully ported, still broken.)
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the useEffect entirely. CategoryForm is mounted fresh each time the dialog opens ({open && <CategoryForm/>} in category-dialog.tsx line 94), so useForm's defaultValues already initialize correctly; the reset-on-render effect is both unnecessary and destructive. If a reset is ever needed, key it on selectedNode?.id/parentNode?.id instead of the object identity.

findings.description: The category/sub_category/item_group filters use raw useURL setters, which do NOT reset the page param — unlike the status filter and search, which go through useListPageState.handleSetFilter/handleSetSearch (hooks/use-list-page-state.ts lines 40-48 call setPage("")). If the user is on page > 1 and applies/changes one of these three filters, the list re-queries with the stale page number and can render an empty page even though matching records exist on page 1.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the three setters to also reset the page, e.g. const setCategoryFilterAndResetPage = (v: string) => { setCategoryFilter(v); handlePageChange/setPage(""); } — or pass an onUpdate option to useURL that clears the page param, mirroring handleSetFilter in use-list-page-state.ts.

findings.description: Error handling only covers desktop list mode: `if (error) return <ErrorState .../>` uses the error from useProduct, which is disabled when isGridMode (mobile or grid toggle). useGridPagination (hooks/use-grid-pagination.ts) never surfaces the underlying query's error — its return object has no error field. So on mobile/grid view a failed product fetch silently renders <EmptyComponent /> ("no products") with no error message or retry, which is misleading.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend useGridPagination to pass through error/refetch from the list hook and render ErrorState in grid mode too (grid.error ? <ErrorState .../> : ...). This affects every module using the hook, so fixing it in the hook benefits all list pages.

findings.description: ProductInfoSummary (entire 186-line file) is dead code — grep over the whole repo finds no import of ProductInfoSummary or pd-info-summary outside the file itself. It was carried over from the source app during migration but pd-form.tsx no longer renders it (its fields were merged into pd-general-tab per the comments there). It also declares props (form, product) it partially ignores.
findings.suggested_fix: Delete routes/product-management/product/_components/pd-info-summary.tsx.

findings.description: Exported getDefaultValues() is never imported anywhere (category-form.tsx, category-dialog.tsx and category-component.tsx import only categorySchema / CategoryFormValues). category-form.tsx lines 55-87 re-implement byte-for-byte identical default-value logic inline. Two copies of the same parent-inheritance logic is a drift hazard — a future change to one will silently diverge from the other.
findings.suggested_fix: Have CategoryForm import and call getDefaultValues(type, selectedNode, parentNode) instead of the inline IIFE, or delete the unused export if the inline version is preferred.

findings.description: Status badge text is hardcoded English: {isActive ? "Active" : "Inactive"}. The desktop list view translates the same status via getProductStatusLabel(ts, ...) (use-product-table.tsx line 143), so Thai users see translated status in the table but English in the mobile/grid cards. The status.active / status.inactive keys already exist in both message files and ts = useTranslations("status") is one line away.
findings.suggested_fix: Add const ts = useTranslations("status") and use {isActive ? ts("active") : ts("inactive")} for the badge.

findings.description: User-facing strings in the image components bypass i18n entirely: toasts (`${valid.length} image(s) uploaded`, "Image deleted", rejection messages lines 57-62), the DeleteDialog title/description ("Delete Image", "Delete this image? This action cannot be undone." lines 207-208), "No images" (line 98), plus pd-image-empty.tsx ("Add product images", "Drop to upload", "Drag & drop or click to browse") and pd-image-lightbox.tsx ("Drop images to upload", "Upload", aria labels). Every other surface in this module uses use-intl, so Thai users get mixed-language UI in the product image workflow.
findings.suggested_fix: Route these strings through useTranslations (e.g. a productManagement.product.images.* namespace) consistent with the rest of the module.

findings.description: ProductEcoLabelSection is rendered without its readOnly prop even when the form mode is "view", so the Add/Edit/Delete eco-label buttons remain fully active (firing API mutations immediately) while every other field on the page is read-only. The component explicitly supports readOnly but no caller passes it. The source Next.js app has the same omission, so this may be intentional independent CRUD — but the unused prop suggests an oversight.
findings.suggested_fix: Pass readOnly={mode === "view"} (or readOnly={isDisabled && !isPending}) to <ProductEcoLabelSection productId={product.id} /> so eco-label mutations follow the form's view/edit gating — or remove the unused readOnly prop if always-editable is intended.

findings.description: The hook's onSubmit option and returned handleSubmit are dead: category-component.tsx line 143 passes onSubmit: () => {} and never destructures handleSubmit — the real submit path is the separate handleFormSubmit wired straight into CategoryDialog. The unused indirection misleads readers into thinking the hook participates in submission.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove onSubmit/handleSubmit from useCategoryDialog and the noop callback at the call site.

findings.description: The tree is rebuilt by an un-memoized IIFE on every render, with subCategories.find()/categories.find() inside the per-item map (O(itemGroups x subCategories) + O(subCategories x categories)). Combined with category-component.tsx passing onInputChange={setSearch} to SearchInput (line 254) — which sets state on every keystroke, bypassing the debounced onSearch — each keypress triggers a full O(n^2) tree rebuild plus the recursive nodeMatches walk. Noticeable jank once the category dataset (fetched with perpage: -1) grows into the thousands.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the tree construction in useMemo([categories, subCategories, itemGroups, isLoading]) and build Map lookups (id -> parent) once instead of find() per child; optionally drop the non-debounced onInputChange and rely on the debounced onSearch.

votes.reason: Cannot refute: defaultValues is rebuilt with new identity every render (IIFE, lines 55-87), so the effect at lines 94-96 re-runs on every re-render; the component subscribes to formState.errors via the RHF proxy (lines 150/163/182), so a failed validation submit re-renders and the effect's form.reset(defaultValues) (default keepErrors:false) wipes user input and clears the errors, and an isPending toggle from the dialog (category-dialog.tsx line 103) does the same — while {open && <CategoryForm/>} (dialog line 94) confirms the fresh-mount premise that makes the effect unnecessary.

Verified, not refuted: the IIFE at category-form.tsx:55-87 creates a new defaultValues identity every render so the effect at lines 94-96 fires on every re-render; the component's form.formState.errors proxy subscription (lines 150/163/182) makes a failed validation submit (trivially reachable via required code/name/tax_profile_id in categorySchema) re-render and immediately reset(), wiping user input and clearing errors (reset defaults to keepErrors:false), and the isPending prop (wired from real TanStack mutations at category-component.tsx:317) toggling on a server error does the same while the dialog stays open — and since category-dialog.tsx:94 mounts CategoryForm fresh per open, the effect is indeed unnecessary, so both the claimed impact and the suggested fix hold.

Cannot refute — a runtime repro (RTL + real react-hook-form 7.75/React 19) shows the lines 94-96 effect is even worse than claimed: because the IIFE-built defaultValues has new identity every render and the component subscribes to form.formState.errors, form.reset fires on every render and the reset→formState-notify→re-render cycle infinite-loops on bare mount ("Maximum update depth exceeded" via the Input maxLength-counter ref at components/ui/input.tsx:25); deleting only the useEffect makes the identical mount test pass, confirming the effect is destructive and the suggested fix (remove it; CategoryForm is freshly mounted via `{open && <CategoryForm/>}` in category-dialog.tsx:94) is sound.

votes.reason: Verified correct: pd-component.tsx:89-91 use raw useURL setters with no onUpdate/page reset, MultiSelectFilter passes values straight through, combinedParams keeps the stale page from useDataGridState, useURL only mutates its own param, and DataGrid has no out-of-range page clamping — so changing category/sub_category/item_group on page > 1 queries with the stale page and can show an empty list, unlike setFilter/setSearch which call setPage("") in use-list-page-state.ts:40-48.

Confirmed real: the three raw useURL setters in pd-component.tsx:89-91 (wired to MultiSelectFilter onChange) only mutate their own query param (use-url.ts:72-97) and never clear `page`, while the stale page flows from useListPageState → useDataGridState params → combinedParams → useProduct, so a user on page > 1 in default desktop list mode who applies/changes a category/sub-category/item-group filter re-queries with the old page number and can see an empty page — exactly the gap the page-resetting handleSetFilter/handleSetSearch (use-list-page-state.ts:40-48) were written to prevent.

Confirmed, not refuted: the three MultiSelectFilters call raw useURL setters (pd-component.tsx:89-91) with no onUpdate, the stale page from the URL flows into combinedParams via useDataGridState's pageNumber, and manualPagination with fully controlled state means nothing resets pageIndex — so changing category/sub_category/item_group while on page > 1 re-queries with the old page and can render an empty list, unlike status/search which reset page via use-list-page-state.ts:40-48.

votes.reason: Verified accurate: useProduct's error (gating ErrorState at pd-component.tsx:270) comes from a query disabled when isGridMode (line 197), useGridPagination destructures only {data, isLoading} and returns no error/refetch (use-grid-pagination.ts:58,119-126), and on a failed grid fetch isLoading goes false with empty items so lines 518-519 silently render EmptyComponent with no global error toast to compensate.

Verified real: useProduct is enabled only when !isGridMode so the error check at pd-component.tsx ~line 270 is dead in grid mode, useGridPagination (hooks/use-grid-pagination.ts) destructures only {data, isLoading} and returns no error/refetch, there is no global QueryCache onError handler in components/providers.tsx, and the grid branch renders EmptyComponent whenever items are empty and not loading — which is exactly the post-error state for any 500/network/429 failure on the easily reachable mobile/grid view.

Confirmed: in grid/mobile mode useProduct is disabled (enabled: !isGridMode) so the line-269 error guard never fires, useGridPagination destructures only {data, isLoading} and exposes no error/refetch, there is no global query error handler (providers.tsx QueryClient has none; http-client only intercepts 401/403/429), so a failed fetch leaves grid.items=[] and isLoading=false, hitting the line-518 branch that renders EmptyComponent — exactly the misleading silent-failure the finding describes.

findings.description: The expired-link branch is dead code. usePriceListExternal calls httpClient.post(), and lib/http-client.ts handleClientErrors() intercepts every 401: it calls refreshTokens(), then tokenStore.clear() and throws ApiError(UNAUTHORIZED, 'Session expired', 401) — never the hook's HttpError. So `error instanceof HttpError && error.status === 401` is always false; an external vendor with an expired token sees a generic ErrorState saying 'Session expired' with a Retry button instead of the intended 'This link has expired' message. The retry predicate in hooks/use-price-list-external.ts (`error instanceof HttpError && error.status === 401`) also fails to match, so the query retries an expired public link 3 times, each attempt invoking the auth refresh flow on a public, unauthenticated page.
findings.suggested_fix: In lib/http-client.ts, skip the 401 refresh/clear handling for /api/external/* URLs (public endpoints), letting the raw response reach the hook's handleResponse(); or in the component/hook, additionally match ApiError with status 401. Also fix the retry predicate accordingly.

findings.description: Race condition: DateControlInner initializes its value with useState(initial), where initial = resolveDateKeyword(node.value, periods). periods comes from the async useReportListLookups query, which is empty ({}) on the dialog's first render. For fields using '@current_period'/'@previous_period', the date locks to the calendar-month fallback computed before the periods arrive, and never updates when the query resolves (useState initializer runs once; there is no effect syncing `initial` to state). Reports get run with wrong period dates on first open.
findings.suggested_fix: Either delay rendering the form until the lookups query (when includePeriods) has resolved, or add an effect in DateControlInner that resets the value when `initial` changes while the field is untouched, or key DateControlInner by the resolved initial value.

findings.description: viewer_endpoint persists API_ENDPOINTS.REPORT_VIEWER(buCode) = '/api/proxy/api/<bu>/reports/viewer' into backend schedule delivery config. '/api/proxy/' is a frontend-internal convention rewritten only inside lib/http-client.ts at request time. In the source Next.js app this path existed as a real server route on the frontend origin; in this static SPA (S3/GCS deploys) no '/api/proxy/*' endpoint exists, so anything that later resolves this stored path (scheduler callbacks, emailed viewer links) against the frontend origin will 404. Only the Docker/nginx deploy proxies /api/*.
findings.suggested_fix: Store a backend-resolvable endpoint instead: send the path the backend understands (e.g. `api/<bu>/reports/viewer` or an absolute URL built from runtime BACKEND_URL), and confirm with the backend team what shape viewer_endpoint must be.

findings.description: Edit mode lets the vendor edit item-level price, moq_qty, price_without_tax, tax_amt and lead_time_days (handleItemFieldChange marks the form dirty), but buildPayload in hooks/use-price-list-external.ts serializes only product_id + moq_tiers (minQuantity/price/leadTimeDays per tier). All item-level field edits are silently dropped on Save, yet form.reset(formData) clears the dirty flag as if everything was persisted. (Behavior is identical in the source Next app, so this may match a backend contract where only MOQ tiers are writable — but then the inputs are misleading.)
findings.suggested_fix: Either include the editable item-level fields in buildPayload (per backend contract), or render those columns read-only in edit mode so only genuinely persistable data (MOQ tiers) appears editable.

findings.description: hasLocalChanges.current is set true on the first local edit and never reset. The sync effect (`if (!hasLocalChanges.current) setLocalTiers(tiers)`) therefore permanently stops syncing from the parent after one edit. After a successful save the query invalidation refetches and form.reset(data) updates the parent tiers (e.g. server-assigned ids replacing the local 'tier-new-<timestamp>' ids), but a mounted sub-table keeps showing/operating on the stale local copies.
findings.suggested_fix: Reset hasLocalChanges.current = false when the tiers prop changes identity after a successful save (or drop the local copy entirely and make the component fully controlled by the parent's moq_tiers via onTiersUpdate).

findings.description: A single pagination/sorting state is shared by both the edit-mode table (one row per detail line) and the view-mode table (grouped rows, fewer pages). Toggling from edit mode while on a later page can leave pageIndex beyond the view table's pageCount, rendering an empty page; sorting state similarly carries over between tables with different column ids.
findings.suggested_fix: Reset pagination (setPagination({pageIndex: 0, pageSize})) when isViewMode toggles, or keep separate pagination state per mode.

findings.description: Delete failure fallback toast uses t('deleteConfirm') — the confirmation-question string — as the error message when err is not an Error instance. The user would see a confirm prompt text in an error toast.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a dedicated failure key (e.g. t('deleteFailed') or toast.deleteFailed) as the fallback message.

findings.description: Group filter is applied client-side to only the current server page (acknowledged in the code comment), but DataGrid still gets recordCount={totalRecords} and pageCount from the unfiltered server response. With a group filter active, the pagination footer reports totals/pages for the unfiltered set while rows show the filtered subset, and reportGroups (the filter's option list) is derived from the current page only — options appear/disappear as the user paginates or searches, and a selected group can vanish from the dropdown.
findings.suggested_fix: Until the backend supports group filtering, pass the filtered count to recordCount when a filter is active and derive group options from a dedicated (unpaginated or cached) groups source; or disable server pagination while a group filter is applied.

findings.description: ReportParamDialogProps declares buCode and ReportComponent passes buCode={buCode}, but the prop is never destructured or used inside ReportParamDialog. Dead prop.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove buCode from ReportParamDialogProps and from the call site in report-component.tsx.

findings.description: parseReportDialog(dialogXml) runs a full DOMParser XML parse on every render of ReportParamDialog (no memoization), and enrichedFields is rebuilt each render too. The dialog re-renders on every lookup-query state change and parent re-render.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the parse in useMemo keyed on dialogXml: const fields = useMemo(() => dialogXml?.trim() ? parseReportDialog(dialogXml) : [], [dialogXml]).

findings.description: GridContent hardcodes English UI strings 'Loading more…' and 'Scroll to load more' instead of using use-intl translations, breaking the th locale on the report history page.
findings.suggested_fix: Add keys (e.g. reportHistory.loadingMore / reportHistory.scrollToLoadMore) to messages/{en,th}.json and use useTranslations in GridContent.

findings.description: FooterBand renders two call-to-action buttons (footerBtnTemplates 'Templates', footerBtnBuilder 'Builder') with no onClick handler and no href/Link — clicking them does nothing. Inert interactive controls on the report landing page.
findings.suggested_fix: Wire them to their destinations (e.g. asChild + Link to /report/list etc.) or remove them until the targets exist.

findings.description: globalThis.window.open(url, '_blank') (fallback at line 113 and toast action at line 120) opens the backend-provided viewer URL without 'noopener,noreferrer'. The opened document gets a window.opener reference back to the authenticated SPA (reverse-tabnabbing surface). Risk is limited since the URL comes from the trusted backend, but other places in the codebase (history-card.tsx) already pass 'noopener,noreferrer'.
findings.suggested_fix: Pass 'noopener,noreferrer' as the third argument to both window.open calls, consistent with history-card.tsx.

confirmed.description: The expired-link branch is dead code. usePriceListExternal calls httpClient.post(), and lib/http-client.ts handleClientErrors() intercepts every 401: it calls refreshTokens(), then tokenStore.clear() and throws ApiError(UNAUTHORIZED, 'Session expired', 401) — never the hook's HttpError. So `error instanceof HttpError && error.status === 401` is always false; an external vendor with an expired token sees a generic ErrorState saying 'Session expired' with a Retry button instead of the intended 'This link has expired' message. The retry predicate in hooks/use-price-list-external.ts (`error instanceof HttpError && error.status === 401`) also fails to match, so the query retries an expired public link 3 times, each attempt invoking the auth refresh flow on a public, unauthenticated page.
confirmed.suggested_fix: In lib/http-client.ts, skip the 401 refresh/clear handling for /api/external/* URLs (public endpoints), letting the raw response reach the hook's handleResponse(); or in the component/hook, additionally match ApiError with status 401. Also fix the retry predicate accordingly.

votes.reason: The core finding is correct and could not be refuted: for the intended user — an unauthenticated external vendor on the public /pl/:url_token page — a 401 from POST /api/external/api/check-pricelist/<token> is intercepted by handleClientErrors() (lib/http-client.ts:214-228), refreshTokens() short-circuits to false (no refresh token in localStorage, lib/auth/auth-api.ts:80-81), and an ApiError(UNAUTHORIZED, 'Session expired', 401) is thrown — ApiError extends Error, not the hook's HttpError, so the `error instanceof HttpError && error.status === 401` branch at routes/external/pl/_components/price-list-external-component.tsx:108 does not match (generic ErrorState with Retry is shown) and the retry predicate at hooks/use-price-list-external.ts:83 also fails, causing 3 retries that each invoke refreshTokens(); the only overstatements are (a) 'dead code'/'always false' — the branch IS reachable when a logged-in user with a valid refresh token opens an expired link, because after a successful refresh handleClientErrors returns the retried raw 401 response un-reintercepted (http-client.ts:224), letting handleResponse throw HttpError(401), and (b) the per-retry 'auth refresh flow' makes no network call for a vendor with no stored refresh token — neither nuance invalidates the high-severity vendor-facing bug.

Verified end-to-end: the backend UrlTokenGuard throws 401 'url_token has expired', lib/http-client.ts handleClientErrors() intercepts every 401 (no /api/external/* exemption) and, for an unauthenticated vendor with no refresh token, throws ApiError('Session expired', 401) which is not an instanceof the hook's HttpError — so the expired-link branch at price-list-external-component.tsx:108 never renders for external vendors and the retry predicate retries the expired link 3 times; only minor overstatements exist (the branch is reachable in the edge case of a logged-in user whose refresh succeeds, since the post-refresh retry bypasses handleClientErrors, and the per-retry refreshTokens() call short-circuits without a network request when no refresh token exists).

Confirmed, not refuted: handleClientErrors() in lib/http-client.ts intercepts 401s on /api/external/* too, and for an unauthenticated external vendor refreshTokens() returns false (no refresh token), so the client throws ApiError("Session expired", 401) before the hook's handleResponse can ever throw HttpError — making the component's `error instanceof HttpError` expired-link branch unreachable and the retry predicate non-matching (3 retries, each invoking the refresh flow); the only path that could produce HttpError(401) is when a valid refresh token exists and the post-refresh retry returns a raw 401, which does not apply to the external-vendor scenario the finding describes.

confirmed.description: Race condition: DateControlInner initializes its value with useState(initial), where initial = resolveDateKeyword(node.value, periods). periods comes from the async useReportListLookups query, which is empty ({}) on the dialog's first render. For fields using '@current_period'/'@previous_period', the date locks to the calendar-month fallback computed before the periods arrive, and never updates when the query resolves (useState initializer runs once; there is no effect syncing `initial` to state). Reports get run with wrong period dates on first open.
confirmed.suggested_fix: Either delay rendering the form until the lookups query (when includePeriods) has resolved, or add an effect in DateControlInner that resets the value when `initial` changes while the field is untouched, or key DateControlInner by the resolved initial value.

votes.reason: The claim is correct: useReportListLookups is a plain async useQuery (no suspense/placeholder), so on first open periods={} and DateControlInner captures the calendar-month fallback in useState(initial) with no syncing effect and no key tied to initial (Field key is the stable field name), so the resolved period start dates never reach the form state until the dialog is closed and reopened with a warm cache.

The finding holds: useReportListLookups is disabled until the dialog opens (no prefetch/cache on first open), the form renders immediately with periods={}, and DateControlInner freezes resolveDateKeyword's calendar-month fallback in useState(initial) with no syncing effect, no key on initial, and a stable Field key — so for @current_period/@previous_period fields the stale fallback date is what FormData submits on first open whenever the accounting period differs from the calendar month.

Not refuted — the race is real: the lookups query is disabled until the report is selected (so periods are always {} on the dialog's first-open render), DateControlInner locks the calendar-month fallback via useState(initial) at routes/report/list/_components/report-param-dialog.tsx:148 with no key or syncing effect (Field key is the stable field name), and the stale value flows through the hidden input into FormData and onRun, so first-open runs of @current_period/@previous_period reports use the fallback date instead of the backend period start.

confirmed.description: Edit mode lets the vendor edit item-level price, moq_qty, price_without_tax, tax_amt and lead_time_days (handleItemFieldChange marks the form dirty), but buildPayload in hooks/use-price-list-external.ts serializes only product_id + moq_tiers (minQuantity/price/leadTimeDays per tier). All item-level field edits are silently dropped on Save, yet form.reset(formData) clears the dirty flag as if everything was persisted. (Behavior is identical in the source Next app, so this may match a backend contract where only MOQ tiers are writable — but then the inputs are misleading.)
confirmed.suggested_fix: Either include the editable item-level fields in buildPayload (per backend contract), or render those columns read-only in edit mode so only genuinely persistable data (MOQ tiers) appears editable.

rejected.description: viewer_endpoint persists API_ENDPOINTS.REPORT_VIEWER(buCode) = '/api/proxy/api/<bu>/reports/viewer' into backend schedule delivery config. '/api/proxy/' is a frontend-internal convention rewritten only inside lib/http-client.ts at request time. In the source Next.js app this path existed as a real server route on the frontend origin; in this static SPA (S3/GCS deploys) no '/api/proxy/*' endpoint exists, so anything that later resolves this stored path (scheduler callbacks, emailed viewer links) against the frontend origin will 404. Only the Docker/nginx deploy proxies /api/*.
rejected.suggested_fix: Store a backend-resolvable endpoint instead: send the path the backend understands (e.g. `api/<bu>/reports/viewer` or an absolute URL built from runtime BACKEND_URL), and confirm with the backend team what shape viewer_endpoint must be.

votes.reason: The sole consumer of viewer_endpoint, micro-cronjobs' resolveViewerEndpoint (internal/executor/report.go:126), explicitly ignores relative paths like '/api/proxy/...' and rebuilds the real URL from REPORT_SERVICE_URL + bu_code (its comment documents this exact frontend proxy-path convention), and notification links use the viewer URL minted by the report service — so nothing ever resolves the stored path against the frontend origin and the predicted 404 cannot happen.

The only consumer of viewer_endpoint, the Go scheduler worker (micro-cronjobs/internal/executor/report.go resolveViewerEndpoint), explicitly ignores any non-absolute value — its comment even names the '/api/proxy/...' frontend path as the expected unusable input — and rebuilds the real URL from REPORT_SERVICE_URL + bu_code, while emailed/web links use the viewer service's response URL, so nothing ever resolves the stored path against the frontend origin and the claimed 404 is unreachable.

The only runtime consumer of viewer_endpoint, micro-cronjobs' resolveViewerEndpoint (internal/executor/report.go:126-144), explicitly ignores non-absolute values like '/api/proxy/...' and rebuilds the URL from REPORT_SERVICE_URL + bu_code, so the stored frontend proxy path is never resolved against the frontend origin and no 404 can manifest (and the value sent is byte-identical to the source Next.js app anyway).

low.description: hasLocalChanges.current is set true on the first local edit and never reset. The sync effect (`if (!hasLocalChanges.current) setLocalTiers(tiers)`) therefore permanently stops syncing from the parent after one edit. After a successful save the query invalidation refetches and form.reset(data) updates the parent tiers (e.g. server-assigned ids replacing the local 'tier-new-<timestamp>' ids), but a mounted sub-table keeps showing/operating on the stale local copies.
low.suggested_fix: Reset hasLocalChanges.current = false when the tiers prop changes identity after a successful save (or drop the local copy entirely and make the component fully controlled by the parent's moq_tiers via onTiersUpdate).

low.description: A single pagination/sorting state is shared by both the edit-mode table (one row per detail line) and the view-mode table (grouped rows, fewer pages). Toggling from edit mode while on a later page can leave pageIndex beyond the view table's pageCount, rendering an empty page; sorting state similarly carries over between tables with different column ids.
low.suggested_fix: Reset pagination (setPagination({pageIndex: 0, pageSize})) when isViewMode toggles, or keep separate pagination state per mode.

low.description: Delete failure fallback toast uses t('deleteConfirm') — the confirmation-question string — as the error message when err is not an Error instance. The user would see a confirm prompt text in an error toast.
low.suggested_fix: Use a dedicated failure key (e.g. t('deleteFailed') or toast.deleteFailed) as the fallback message.

low.description: Group filter is applied client-side to only the current server page (acknowledged in the code comment), but DataGrid still gets recordCount={totalRecords} and pageCount from the unfiltered server response. With a group filter active, the pagination footer reports totals/pages for the unfiltered set while rows show the filtered subset, and reportGroups (the filter's option list) is derived from the current page only — options appear/disappear as the user paginates or searches, and a selected group can vanish from the dropdown.
low.suggested_fix: Until the backend supports group filtering, pass the filtered count to recordCount when a filter is active and derive group options from a dedicated (unpaginated or cached) groups source; or disable server pagination while a group filter is applied.

findings.description: The xlsx dependency is fetched as a raw URL tarball from cdn.sheetjs.com, and its bun.lock entry (bun.lock line 1093) has NO integrity hash — unlike every registry dependency, which carries a sha512. Every `bun install` (including the Docker build, which runs `bun install --frozen-lockfile` on a clean container) re-downloads the tarball with no checksum verification. If the SheetJS CDN is compromised or serves different content, the altered code is silently accepted into the production bundle. This is a supply-chain gap in an ERP frontend that handles auth tokens in the same JS context.
findings.suggested_fix: Pin with verification: vendor the tarball into the repo (e.g. `vendor/xlsx-0.20.3.tgz` referenced as a `file:` dependency) after checking its published checksum, or add a postinstall/CI step that verifies the sha512 of node_modules/xlsx against a committed expected hash.

findings.description: `aws s3 sync --delete` (and `--delete-unmatched-destination-objects` in deploy-gcs.sh:23) removes all previous hashed chunks the moment a new build is synced. Every route in routes/router.tsx is a `lazy:` module, and the app has no stale-chunk recovery (no `vite:preloadError` listener, no reload-on-chunk-failure anywhere in main.tsx/routes/components/lib — verified by grep). So any user with an open session at deploy time gets a hard dynamic-import failure (error boundary at best) on the next navigation to a not-yet-visited route, until they manually reload. For a long-session ERP this will happen on essentially every deploy.
findings.suggested_fix: Either keep previous-build assets for a grace period (drop --delete and garbage-collect assets/ older than N days in a separate step), or add a global `window.addEventListener("vite:preloadError", () => window.location.reload())` in main.tsx so stale sessions self-heal; ideally both.

findings.description: The S3 sync applies `Cache-Control: public,max-age=31536000,immutable` to EVERYTHING in dist/ except index.html and config*.json — not just hashed assets. Today dist/ only contains assets/ + index.html so it is latent, but the moment anything is added to public/ (favicon file, robots.txt, manifest, images — likely as the port matures), that un-hashed file gets a 1-year immutable cache and can never be updated for existing visitors. deploy-gcs.sh already does this correctly (immutable only on assets/**).
findings.suggested_fix: Mirror the GCS script: sync with a safe default Cache-Control (e.g. no-cache or short max-age), then re-apply the immutable header only to `assets/*` (aws s3 cp dist/assets s3://$BUCKET/assets --recursive --cache-control "public,max-age=31536000,immutable" or a second sync scoped to assets/).

findings.description: X_APP_ID and WS_URL are interpolated into config.json via printf %s with no JSON escaping. A value containing a double-quote or backslash produces invalid JSON, and the SPA then fails at boot inside `loadRuntimeConfig()` with an opaque parse error rather than a clear container-start failure. Values are operator-controlled (UUID/URL) so it is unlikely, but the failure mode is confusing when it happens.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate the values at render time (reject characters outside a safe set, e.g. `case "$X_APP_ID" in *[\"\\]*) echo 'invalid X_APP_ID' >&2; exit 1;; esac`), or render the JSON with a tool that escapes (jq is not in nginx:alpine, so validation is the cheap route). Exiting non-zero from a /docker-entrypoint.d script aborts nginx startup, which is the desired fail-fast.

findings.description: Neither the render script nor docker/nginx.conf.template guards against BACKEND_URL having a trailing slash. With `proxy_pass ${BACKEND_URL};` (nginx.conf.template:32), a value like `https://backend/` makes proxy_pass carry a URI part, so nginx replaces the matched `location /api/` prefix: `/api/auth/login` is forwarded as `/auth/login`, silently breaking every API call. With no trailing slash (the documented form) the full URI is passed unchanged and everything works — so this is a one-character misconfiguration that produces a total, hard-to-diagnose failure.
findings.suggested_fix: Fail fast in 40-render-config-json.sh (it runs via the same set -e entrypoint): `case "$BACKEND_URL" in */) echo "BACKEND_URL must not end with /" >&2; exit 1;; esac`. (Stripping it in the script is not enough — 20-envsubst-on-templates.sh has already rendered nginx.conf from the raw env by then, so validate-and-abort is the correct fix.)

findings.description: vite.config.ts compiles all components through babel-plugin-react-compiler, but vitest.config.ts uses a plain `react()` plugin. Tests therefore exercise un-compiled components while production ships compiler-transformed (auto-memoized) code. Compiler-surfaced bugs — impure render functions, mutation of props/state that auto-memoization makes observable — pass tests and only appear in the built app.
findings.suggested_fix: Use the same plugin options in vitest.config.ts: `react({ babel: { plugins: ["babel-plugin-react-compiler"] } })`, or better, have vitest.config.ts `mergeConfig` the real vite.config.ts so plugin/alias config cannot drift.

findings.description: The nginx vhost enables gzip but sets no `gzip_vary on`, so any intermediary cache may serve gzipped bodies to clients that did not send Accept-Encoding. It also sends no security headers at all — no X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, no frame-ancestors/X-Frame-Options (clickjacking on an ERP login page), no Referrer-Policy. The S3/CloudFront path has the same gap but that belongs to CDN config; the Docker image fully controls its headers here.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the server block: `gzip_vary on;`, `add_header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff always;`, `add_header X-Frame-Options DENY always;` (or a CSP with frame-ancestors 'none'), `add_header Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin always;`. Note add_header inheritance: headers added in the server block are suppressed in locations that declare their own add_header (assets/, config.json, /), so repeat them or use a shared include.

findings.description: The dev proxy hardcodes `secure: false`, disabling TLS certificate verification for whatever VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET points at — including when developers proxy to the real UAT/dev backend (public/config.json points at https://dev.blueledgers.com:4001) while sending live credentials through it. This is dev-only and deliberate for self-signed UAT certs, but it unconditionally applies to all targets.
findings.suggested_fix: Gate it on an explicit opt-in, e.g. `secure: process.env.VITE_DEV_PROXY_INSECURE !== "1"`, so cert verification stays on by default and is only disabled when a developer knowingly targets a self-signed backend.

findings.description: The generateMetadata stripper only matches `generateMetadata()` with literally empty parens and at most one level of nested braces in the body. Next's `generateMetadata({ params })` signature, or a body with two-level nesting (e.g. `return { title: t("x", { ns: "y" }) }`), is left untouched, leaving a dangling function referencing the removed `Metadata` import — caught later only by tsc/ESLint. Per CLAUDE.md all sections are already migrated, so this is a residual-tooling robustness note for future module ports, not a live bug.
findings.suggested_fix: Broaden the regex: `s/export async function generateMetadata\([^)]*\)[^{]*\{(?:[^{}]|\{(?:[^{}]|\{[^{}]*\})*\})*\}\n+//s` (params + one more nesting level), and have the script grep for any surviving `generateMetadata`/`Metadata` afterwards and emit the same WARN it already uses for missing default exports.

The claimed impact is overstated: the app does have a designed stale-chunk recovery path the reviewer's grep missed — routes/module-error-boundary-adapter.tsx resets via navigate(0) (a full reload, explicitly commented as re-fetching failed chunks) and is registered on every module section in routes/router.tsx, so stale sessions self-heal with one click on the retry button (index.html is no-cache and CDN-invalidated); additionally, sync --delete only removes chunks whose content hash changed and old immutable chunks persist in CDN edge caches, shrinking the failure window from "every deploy, hard failure" to a one-click-recoverable transient error.

Tests therefore exercise un-compiled components while production ships compiler-transformed (auto-memoized) code. Compiler-surfaced bugs — impure render functions, mutation of props/state that auto-memoization makes observable — pass tests and only appear in the built app.
low.suggested_fix: Use the same plugin options in vitest.config.ts: `react({ babel: { plugins: ["babel-plugin-react-compiler"] } })`, or better, have vitest.config.ts `mergeConfig` the real vite.config.ts so plugin/alias config cannot drift.

low.description: The nginx vhost enables gzip but sets no `gzip_vary on`, so any intermediary cache may serve gzipped bodies to clients that did not send Accept-Encoding. It also sends no security headers at all — no X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, no frame-ancestors/X-Frame-Options (clickjacking on an ERP login page), no Referrer-Policy. The S3/CloudFront path has the same gap but that belongs to CDN config; the Docker image fully controls its headers here.
low.suggested_fix: Add to the server block: `gzip_vary on;`, `add_header X-Content-Type-Options nosniff always;`, `add_header X-Frame-Options DENY always;` (or a CSP with frame-ancestors 'none'), `add_header Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin always;`. Note add_header inheritance: headers added in the server block are suppressed in locations that declare their own add_header (assets/, config.json, /), so repeat them or use a shared include.

low.description: The dev proxy hardcodes `secure: false`, disabling TLS certificate verification for whatever VITE_DEV_PROXY_TARGET points at — including when developers proxy to the real UAT/dev backend (public/config.json points at https://dev.blueledgers.com:4001) while sending live credentials through it. This is dev-only and deliberate for self-signed UAT certs, but it unconditionally applies to all targets.
low.suggested_fix: Gate it on an explicit opt-in, e.g. `secure: process.env.VITE_DEV_PROXY_INSECURE !== "1"`, so cert verification stays on by default and is only disabled when a developer knowingly targets a self-signed backend.

low.description: The generateMetadata stripper only matches `generateMetadata()` with literally empty parens and at most one level of nested braces in the body. Next's `generateMetadata({ params })` signature, or a body with two-level nesting (e.g. `return { title: t("x", { ns: "y" }) }`), is left untouched, leaving a dangling function referencing the removed `Metadata` import — caught later only by tsc/ESLint. Per CLAUDE.md all sections are already migrated, so this is a residual-tooling robustness note for future module ports, not a live bug.
low.suggested_fix: Broaden the regex: `s/export async function generateMetadata\([^)]*\)[^{]*\{(?:[^{}]|\{(?:[^{}]|\{[^{}]*\})*\})*\}\n+//s` (params + one more nesting level), and have the script grep for any surviving `generateMetadata`/`Metadata` afterwards and emit the same WARN it already uses for missing default exports.
